return; }\n 2831 | \n 2832 |   /* Skip fixed header */\n 2833 |   uint pos = 19;\n 2834 | \n 2835 |   /*\n 2836 |    *\tUPDATE message format\n 2837 |    *\n 2838 |    *\t2 B\tIPv4 Withdrawn Routes Length\n 2839 |    *\tvar\tIPv4 Withdrawn Routes NLRI\n 2840 |    *\t2 B\tTotal Path Attribute Length\n 2841 |    *\tvar\tPath Attributes\n 2842 |    *\tvar\tIPv4 Reachable Routes NLRI\n 2843 |    */\n 2844 | \n 2845 |   s.ip_unreach_len = get_u16(pkt + pos);\n 2846 |   s.ip_unreach_nlri = pkt + pos + 2;\n 2847 |   pos += 2 + s.ip_unreach_len;\n 2848 | \n 2849 |   if (pos + 2 > len)\n 2850 |     bgp_parse_error(&s, 1);\n 2851 | \n 2852 |   s.attr_len = get_u16(pkt + pos);\n 2853 |   s.attrs = pkt + pos + 2;\n 2854 |   pos += 2 + s.attr_len;\n 2855 | \n 2856 |   if (pos > len)\n 2857 |     bgp_parse_error(&s, 1);\n 2858 | \n 2859 |   s.ip_reach_len = len - pos;\n 2860 |   s.ip_reach_nlri = pkt + pos;\n 2861 | \n 2862 |   if (s.attr_len)\n 2863 |     ea = bgp_decode_attrs(&s, s.attrs, s.attr_len);\n 2864 |   else\n 2865 |     ea = NULL;\n 2866 | \n 2867 |   /* Check for End-of-RIB marker */\n 2868 |   if (!s.attr_len && !s.ip_unreach_len && !s.ip_reach_len)\n 2869 |   { bgp_rx_end_mark(&s, BGP_AF_IPV4); goto done; }\n 2870 | \n 2871 |   /* Check for MP End-of-RIB marker */\n 2872 |   if ((s.attr_len < 8) && !s.ip_unreach_len && !s.ip_reach_len &&\n 2873 |       !s.mp_reach_len && !s.mp_unreach_len && s.mp_unreach_af)\n 2874 |   { bgp_rx_end_mark(&s, s.mp_unreach_af); goto done; }\n 2875 | \n 2876 |   if (s.ip_unreach_len)\n 2877 |     bgp_decode_nlri(&s, BGP_AF_IPV4, s.ip_unreach_nlri, s.ip_unreach_len, NULL, NULL, 0);\n 2878 | \n 2879 |   if (s.mp_unreach_len)\n 2880 |     bgp_decode_nlri(&s, s.mp_unreach_af, s.mp_unreach_nlri, s.mp_unreach_len, NULL, NULL, 0);\n 2881 | \n 2882 |   s.reach_nlri_step = 1;\n 2883 | \n 2884 |   if (s.ip_reach_len)\n 2885 |     bgp_decode_nlri(&s, BGP_AF_IPV4, s.ip_reach_nlri, s.ip_reach_len,\n 2886 | \t\t    ea, s.ip_next_hop_data, s.ip_next_hop_len);\n 2887 | \n 2888 |   if (s.mp_reach_len)\n 2889 |     bgp_decode_nlri(&s, s.mp_reach_af, s.mp_reach_nlri, s.mp_reach_len,\n 2890 | \t\t    ea, s.mp_next_hop_data, s.mp_next_hop_len);\n 2891 | \n 2892 | done:\n 2893 |   rta_free(s.cached_rta);\n 2894 |   lp_restore(tmp_linpool, &tmpp);\n 2895 |   return;\n 2896 | }\n 2897 | \n 2898 | static uint\n 2899 | bgp_find_update_afi(byte *pos, uint len)\n 2900 | {\n 2901 |   /*\n 2902 |    * This is stripped-down version of bgp_rx_update(), bgp_decode_attrs() and\n 2903 |    * bgp_decode_mp_[un]reach_nlri() used by MRT code in order to find out which\n 2904 |    * AFI/SAFI is associated with incoming UPDATE. Returns 0 for framing errors.\n 2905 |    */\n 2906 |   if (len < 23)\n 2907 |     return 0;\n 2908 | \n 2909 |   /* Assume there is no withrawn NLRI, read lengths and move to attribute list */\n 2910 |   uint wlen = get_u16(pos + 19);\n 2911 |   uint alen = get_u16(pos + 21);\n 2912 |   ADVANCE(pos, len, 23);\n 2913 | \n 2914 |   /* Either non-zero withdrawn NLRI, non-zero reachable NLRI, or IPv4 End-of-RIB */\n 2915 |   if ((wlen != 0) || (alen < len) || !alen)\n 2916 |     return BGP_AF_IPV4;\n 2917 | \n 2918 |   if (alen > len)\n 2919 |     return 0;\n 2920 | \n 2921 |   /* Process attribute list (alen == len) */\n 2922 |   while (len)\n 2923 |   {\n 2924 |     if (len < 2)\n 2925 |       return 0;\n 2926 | \n 2927 |     uint flags = pos[0];\n 2928 |     uint code = pos[1];\n 2929 |     ADVANCE(pos, len, 2);\n 2930 | \n 2931 |     uint ll = !(flags & BAF_EXT_LEN) ? 1 : 2;\n 2932 |     if (len < ll)\n 2933 |       return 0;\n 2934 | \n 2935 |     /* Read attribute length and move to attribute body */\n 2936 |     alen = (ll == 1) ? get_u8(pos) : get_u16(pos);\n 2937 |     ADVANCE(pos, len, ll);\n 2938 | \n 2939 |     if (len < alen)\n 2940 |       return 0;\n 2941 | \n 2942 |     /* Found MP NLRI */\n 2943 |     if ((code == BA_MP_REACH_NLRI) || (code == BA_MP_UNREACH_NLRI))\n 2944 |     {\n 2945 |       if (alen < 3)\n 2946 | \treturn 0;\n 2947 | \n 2948 |       return BGP_AF(get_u16(pos), pos[2]);\n 2949 |     }\n 2950 | \n 2951 |     /* Move to the next attribute */\n 2952 |     ADVANCE(pos, len, alen);\n 2953 |   }\n 2954 | \n 2955 |   /* No basic or MP NLRI, but there are some attributes -> error */\n 2956 |   return 0;\n 2957 | }\n 2958 | \n 2959 | \n 2960 | /*\n 2961 |  *\tROUTE-REFRESH\n 2962 |  */\n 2963 | \n 2964 | static inline byte *\n 2965 | bgp_create_route_refresh(struct bgp_channel *c, byte *buf)\n 2966 | {\n 2967 |   struct bgp_proto *p = (void *) c->c.proto;\n 2968 | \n 2969 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Sending ROUTE-REFRESH\");\n 2970 | \n 2971 |   /* Original route refresh request, RFC 2918 */\n 2972 |   put_af4(buf, c->afi);\n 2973 |   buf[2] = BGP_RR_REQUEST;\n 2974 | \n 2975 |   return buf+4;\n 2976 | }\n 2977 | \n 2978 | static inline byte *\n 2979 | bgp_create_begin_refresh(struct bgp_channel *c, byte *buf)\n 2980 | {\n 2981 |   struct bgp_proto *p = (void *) c->c.proto;\n 2982 | \n 2983 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Sending BEGIN-OF-RR\");\n 2984 | \n 2985 |   /* Demarcation of beginning of route refresh (BoRR), RFC 7313 */\n 2986 |   put_af4(buf, c->afi);\n 2987 |   buf[2] = BGP_RR_BEGIN;\n 2988 | \n 2989 |   return buf+4;\n 2990 | }\n 2991 | \n 2992 | static inline byte *\n 2993 | bgp_create_end_refresh(struct bgp_channel *c, byte *buf)\n 2994 | {\n 2995 |   struct bgp_proto *p = (void *) c->c.proto;\n 2996 | \n 2997 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Sending END-OF-RR\");\n 2998 | \n 2999 |   /* Demarcation of ending of route refresh (EoRR), RFC 7313 */\n 3000 |   put_af4(buf, c->afi);\n 3001 |   buf[2] = BGP_RR_END;\n 3002 | \n 3003 |   return buf+4;\n 3004 | }\n 3005 | \n 3006 | static void\n 3007 | bgp_rx_route_refresh(struct bgp_conn *conn, byte *pkt, uint len)\n 3008 | {\n 3009 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n 3010 | \n 3011 |   if (conn->state != BS_ESTABLISHED)\n 3012 |   { bgp_error(conn, 5, fsm_err_subcode[conn->state], NULL, 0); return; }\n 3013 | \n 3014 |   if (!conn->local_caps->route_refresh)\n 3015 |   { bgp_error(conn, 1, 3, pkt+18, 1); return; }\n 3016 | \n 3017 |   if (len < (BGP_HEADER_LENGTH + 4))\n 3018 |   { bgp_error(conn, 1, 2, pkt+16, 2); return; }\n 3019 | \n 3020 |   if (len > (BGP_HEADER_LENGTH + 4))\n 3021 |   { bgp_error(conn, 7, 1, pkt, MIN(len, 2048)); return; }\n 3022 | \n 3023 |   struct bgp_channel *c = bgp_get_channel(p, |     return;\n 3029 |   }\n 3030 | \n 3031 |   /* RFC 7313 redefined reserved field as RR message subtype */\n 3032 |   uint subtype = p->enhanced_refresh ? pkt[21] : BGP_RR_REQUEST;\n 3033 | \n 3034 |   switch (subtype)\n 3035 |   {\n 3036 |   case BGP_RR_REQUEST:\n 3037 |     BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Got ROUTE-REFRESH\");\n 3038 |     channel_request_feeding(&c->c);\n 3039 |     break;\n 3040 | \n 3041 |   case BGP_RR_BEGIN:\n 3042 |     BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Got BEGIN-OF-RR\");\n 3043 |     bgp_refresh_begin(c);\n 3044 |     break;\n 3045 | \n 3046 |   case BGP_RR_END:\n 3047 | %u, ignoring\",\n 3053 | \tp->p.name, subtype);\n 3054 |     break;\n 3055 |   }\n 3056 | }\n 3057 | \n 3058 | static inline struct bgp_channel *\n 3059 | bgp_get_channel_to_send(struct bgp_proto *p, struct bgp_conn *conn)\n 3060 | {\n 3061 |   uint i = conn->last_channel;\n 3062 | \n 3063 |   /* Try the last channel, but at most several times */\n 3064 |   if ((conn->channels_to_send & (1 << i)) &&\n 3065 |       (conn->last_channel_count < 16))\n 3066 |     goto found;\n 3067 | \n 3068 |   /* Find channel with non-zero channels_to_send */\n 3069 |   do\n 3070 |   {\n 3071 |     i++;\n 3072 |     if (i >= p->channel_count)\n 3073 |       i = 0;\n 3074 |   }\n 3075 |   while (! (conn->channels_to_send & (1 << i)));\n 3076 | \n 3077 |   /* Use that channel */\n 3078 |   conn->last_channel = i;\n 3079 |   conn->last_channel_count = 0;\n 3080 | \n 3081 | found:\n 3082 |   conn->last_channel_count++;\n 3083 |   return p->channel_map[i];\n 3084 | }\n 3085 | \n 3086 | static inline int\n 3087 | bgp_send(struct bgp_conn *conn, uint type, uint len)\n 3088 | {\n 3089 |   sock *sk = conn->sk;\n 3090 |   byte *buf = sk->tbuf;\n 3091 | \n 3092 |   conn->bgp->stats.tx_messages++;\n 3093 |   conn->bgp->stats.tx_bytes += len;\n 3094 | \n 3095 |   memset(buf, 0xff, BGP_HDR_MARKER_LENGTH);\n 3096 |   put_u16(buf+16, len);\n 3097 |   buf[18] = type;\n 3098 | \n 3099 |   int success = sk_send(sk, len);\n 3100 |   if (success && ((conn->state == BS_ESTABLISHED) || (conn->state == BS_OPENCONFIRM)))\n 3101 |     bgp_start_timer(conn->send_hold_timer, conn->send_hold_time);\n 3102 | \n 3103 |   return success;\n 3104 | }\n 3105 | \n 3106 | /**\n 3107 |  * bgp_fire_tx - transmit packets\n 3108 |  * @conn: connection\n 3109 |  *\n 3110 |  * Whenever the transmit buffers of the underlying TCP connection\n 3111 |  * are free and we have any packets queued for sending, the socket functions\n 3112 |  * call bgp_fire_tx() which takes care of selecting the highest priority packet\n 3113 |  * queued (Notification > Keepalive > Open > Update), assembling its header\n 3114 |  * and body and sending it to the connection.\n 3115 |  */\n 3116 | static int\n 3117 | bgp_fire_tx(struct bgp_conn *conn)\n 3118 | {\n 3119 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n 3120 |   struct bgp_channel *c;\n 3121 |   byte *buf, *pkt, *end;\n 3122 |   uint s;\n 3123 | \n 3124 |   if (!conn->sk)\n 3125 |     return 0;\n 3126 | \n 3127 |   buf = conn->sk->tbuf;\n 3128 |   pkt = buf + BGP_HEADER_LENGTH;\n 3129 |   s = conn->packets_to_send;\n 3130 | \n 3131 |   if (s & (1 << PKT_SCHEDULE_CLOSE))\n 3132 |   {\n 3133 |     /* We can finally close connection and enter idle state */\n 3134 |     bgp_conn_enter_idle_state(conn);\n 3135 |     return 0;\n 3136 |   }\n 3137 |   if (s & (1 << PKT_NOTIFICATION))\n 3138 |   {\n 3139 |     conn->packets_to_send = 1 << PKT_SCHEDULE_CLOSE;\n 3140 |     end = bgp_create_notification(conn, pkt);\n 3141 |     return bgp_send(conn, PKT_NOTIFICATION, end - buf);\n 3142 |   }\n 3143 |   else if (s & (1 << PKT_OPEN))\n 3144 |   {\n 3145 |     conn->packets_to_send &= ~(1 << PKT_OPEN);\n 3146 |     end = bgp_create_open(conn, pkt);\n 3147 | \n 3148 |     conn->local_open_msg = bgp_copy_open(p, buf, end - buf);\n 3149 |     conn->local_open_length = end - buf - BGP_HEADER_LENGTH;\n 3150 | \n 3151 |     return bgp_send(conn, PKT_OPEN, end - buf);\n 3152 |   }\n 3153 |   else if (s & (1 << PKT_KEEPALIVE))\n 3154 |   {\n 3155 |     conn->packets_to_send &= ~(1 << PKT_KEEPALIVE);\n 3156 |     BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Sending KEEPALIVE\");\n 3157 |     bgp_start_timer(conn->keepalive_timer, conn->keepalive_time);\n 3158 |     return bgp_send(conn, PKT_KEEPALIVE, BGP_HEADER_LENGTH);\n 3159 |   }\n 3160 |   else while (conn->channels_to_send)\n 3161 |   {\n 3162 |     c = bgp_get_channel_to_send(p, conn);\n 3163 |     s = c->packets_to_send;\n 3164 | \n 3165 |     if (s & (1 << PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH))\n 3166 |     {\n 3167 |       c->packets_to_send &= ~(1 << PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH);\n 3168 |       end = bgp_create_route_refresh(c, pkt);\n 3169 |       return bgp_send(conn, PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH, end - buf);\n 3170 |     }\n 3171 |     else if (s & (1 << PKT_BEGIN_REFRESH))\n 3172 |     {\n 3173 |       /* BoRR is a subtype of RR, but uses separate bit in packets_to_send */\n 3174 |       c->packets_to_send &= ~(1 << PKT_BEGIN_REFRESH);\n 3175 |       end = bgp_create_begin_refresh(c, pkt);\n 3176 |       return bgp_send(conn, PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH, end - buf);\n 3177 |     }\n 3178 |     else if (s & (1 << PKT_UPDATE))\n 3179 |     {\n 3180 |       end = bgp_create_update(c, pkt);\n 3181 |       if (end)\n 3182 | \treturn bgp_send(conn, PKT_UPDATE, end - buf);\n 3183 | \n 3184 |       /* No update to send, perhaps we need to send End-of-RIB or EoRR */\n 3185 |       c->packets_to_send = 0;\n 3186 |       conn->channels_to_send &= ~(1 << c->index);\n 3187 | \n 3188 |       if (c->feed_state == BFS_LOADED)\n 3189 |       {\n 3190 | \tc->feed_state = BFS_NONE;\n 3191 | \tend = bgp_create_end_mark(c, pkt);\n 3192 | \treturn bgp_send(conn, PKT_UPDATE, end - buf);\n 3193 |       }\n 3194 | \n 3195 |       else if (c->feed_state == BFS_REFRESHED)\n 3196 |       {\n 3197 | \tc->feed_state = BFS_NONE;\n 3198 | \tend = bgp_create_end_refresh(c, pkt);\n 3199 | \treturn bgp_send(conn, PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH, end - buf);\n 3200 |       }\n 3201 |     }\n 3202 |     else if (s)\n 3203 |       bug(\"Channel packets_to_send: %x\", s);\n 3204 | \n 3205 |     c->packets_to_send = 0;\n 3206 |     conn->channels_to_send &= ~(1 << c->index);\n 3207 |   }\n 3208 | \n 3209 |   return 0;\n 3210 | }\n 3211 | \n 3212 | /**\n 3213 |  * bgp_schedule_packet - schedule a packet for transmission\n 3214 |  * @conn: connection\n 3215 |  * @c: channel\n 3216 |  * @type: packet type\n 3217 |  *\n 3218 |  * Schedule a packet of type @type to be sent as soon as possible.\n 3219 |  */\n 3220 | void\n 3221 | bgp_schedule_packet(struct bgp_conn *conn, struct bgp_channel *c, int type)\n 3222 | {\n 3223 |   ASSERT(conn->sk);\n 3224 | \n 3225 |   DBG(\"BGP: Scheduling packet type %d\\n\", type);\n 3226 | \n 3227 |   if (c)\n 3228 |   {\n 3229 |     if (! conn->channels_to_send)\n 3230 | |= 1 << c->index;\n 3237 |   }\n 3238 |   else\n 3239 |     conn->packets_to_send |= 1 << type;\n 3240 | \n 3241 |   if ((conn->sk->tpos == conn->sk->tbuf) && !ev_active(conn->tx_ev))\n 3242 | 1024;\n 3269 |   while (--max && (bgp_fire_tx(conn) > 0))\n 3270 |     ;\n 3271 | \n 3272 |   if (!max && !ev_active(conn->tx_ev))\n 3273 |     ev_schedule(conn->tx_ev);\n 3274 | }\n 3275 | \n 3276 | |  * bgp_error_dsc - return BGP error description\n 3328 |  * @code: BGP error code\n 3329 |  * @subcode: BGP error subcode\n 3330 |  *\n 3331 |  * bgp_error_dsc() returns error description for BGP errors\n 3332 |  * which might be static string or given temporary buffer.\n 3333 |  */\n 3334 | const char *\n 3335 | bgp_error_dsc(uint code, uint subcode)\n 3336 | {\n 3337 |   static char buff[32];\n 3338 |   uint i;\n 3339 | \n 3340 |   for (i=0; i < ARRAY_SIZE(bgp_msg_table); i++)\n 3341 |     if (bgp_msg_table[i].major == code && bgp_msg_table[i].minor == subcode)\n 3342 |       return bgp_msg_table[i].msg;\n 3343 | \n 3344 |   bsprintf(buff, \"Unknown error %u.%u\", code, subcode);\n 3345 |   return buff;\n 3346 | }\n 3347 | \n 3348 | /* RFC 9003 - shutdown communication message */\n 3349 | static int\n 3350 | bgp_handle_message(struct bgp_proto *p, byte *data, uint len, byte **bp)\n 3351 | {\n 3352 |   byte *msg = data + 1;\n 3353 |   uint msg_len = data[0];\n 3354 |   uint i;\n 3355 | \n 3356 |   /* Handle zero length message */\n 3357 |   if (msg_len == 0)\n 3358 |     return 1;\n 3359 | \n 3360 |   /* Handle proper message */\n 3361 |   if (msg_len + 1 > len)\n 3362 |     return 0;\n 3363 | \n 3364 |   /* Some elementary cleanup */\n 3365 |   for (i = 0; i < msg_len; i++)\n 3366 |     if (msg[i] < ' ')\n 3367 |       msg[i] = ' ';\n 3368 | \n 3369 |   proto_set_message(&p->p, msg, msg_len);\n 3370 |   *bp += bsprintf(*bp, \": \\\"%s\\\"\", p->p.message);\n 3371 |   return 1;\n 3372 | }\n 3373 | \n 3374 | void\n 3375 | bgp_log_error(struct bgp_proto *p, u8 class, char *msg, uint code, uint subcode, byte *data, uint len)\n 3376 | {\n 3377 |   byte argbuf[256+16], *t = argbuf;\n 3378 |   uint i;\n 3379 | \n 3380 |   /* Don't report Cease messages generated by myself */\n 3381 |   if (code == 6 && class == BE_BGP_TX)\n 3382 |     return;\n 3383 | \n 3384 |   /* Reset shutdown message */\n 3385 |   if ((code == 6) && ((subcode == 2) || (subcode == 4)))\n 3386 |     proto_set_message(&p->p, NULL, 0);\n 3387 | \n 3388 |   if (len)\n 3389 |     {\n 3390 |       /* Bad peer AS / unacceptable hold time - print the value as decimal number */\n 3391 |       if ((code == 2) && ((subcode == 2) || (subcode == 6)) && ((len == 2) || (len == 4)))\n 3392 | \t{\n 3393 | \t  t += bsprintf(t, \": %u\", (len == 2) ? get_u16(data) : get_u32(data));\n 3394 | \t  goto done;\n 3395 | \t}\n 3396 | \n 3397 |       if ((code == 2) && (subcode == 11) && (len == 1))\n 3398 |         {\n 3399 | \t  t += bsprintf(t, \" (%s)\", bgp_format_role_name(get_u8(data)));\n 3400 | \t  goto done;\n 3401 |         }\n 3402 | \n 3403 |       /* RFC 9003 - shutdown communication */\n 3404 |       if (((code == 6) && ((subcode == 2) || (subcode == 4))))\n 3405 | \tif (bgp_handle_message(p, data, len, &t))\n 3406 | \t  goto done;\n 3407 | \n 3408 |       *t++ = ':';\n 3409 |       *t++ = ' ';\n 3410 |       if (len > 128)\n 3411 | \tlen = 128;\n 3412 |       for (i=0; i<len; i++)\n 3413 | \tt += bsprintf(t, \"%02x\", data[i]);\n 3414 |     }\n 3415 | \n 3416 | done:\n 3417 |   *t = 0;\n 3418 |   const byte *dsc = bgp_error_dsc(code, subcode);\n 3419 |   log(L_REMOTE \"%s: %s: %s%s\", p->p.name, msg, dsc, argbuf);\n 3420 | }\n 3421 | \n 3422 | static void\n 3423 | bgp_rx_notification(struct bgp_conn *conn, byte *pkt, uint len)\n 3424 | {\n 3425 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n 3426 | \n 3427 |   if (len < 21)\n 3428 |   { bgp_error(conn, 1, 2, pkt+16, 2); return; }\n 3429 | \n 3430 |   uint code = pkt[19];\n 3431 |   uint subcode = pkt[20];\n 3432 |   int err = (code != 6);\n 3433 | \n 3434 |   bgp_log_error(p, BE_BGP_RX, \"Received\", code, subcode, pkt+21, len-21);\n 3435 |   bgp_store_error(p, conn, BE_BGP_RX, (code << 16) | subcode);\n 3436 | \n 3437 |   conn->notify_code = code;\n 3438 |   conn->notify_subcode = subcode;\n 3439 |   conn->notify_data = pkt+21;\n 3440 |   conn->notify_size = len-21;\n 3441 | \n 3442 |   bgp_conn_enter_close_state(conn);\n 3443 |   bgp_schedule_packet(conn, NULL, PKT_SCHEDULE_CLOSE);\n 3444 | \n 3445 |   if (err)\n 3446 |   {\n 3447 |     bgp_update_startup_delay(p);\n 3448 |     bgp_stop(p, 0, NULL, 0);\n 3449 |   }\n 3450 |   else\n 3451 |   {\n 3452 |     uint subcode_bit = 1 << ((subcode <= 8) ? subcode : 0);\n 3453 |     if (p->cf->disable_after_cease & subcode_bit)\n 3454 |     {\n 3455 |       log(L_INFO \"%s: Disabled after Cease notification\", p->p.name);\n 3456 |       p->startup_delay = 0;\n 3457 |       p->p.disabled = 1;\n 3458 |     }\n 3459 |   }\n 3460 | }\n 3461 | \n 3462 | static void\n 3463 | bgp_rx_keepalive(struct bgp_conn *conn)\n 3464 | {\n 3465 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n 3466 | \n 3467 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Got KEEPALIVE\");\n 3468 |   bgp_start_timer(conn->hold_timer, conn->hold_time);\n 3469 | \n 3470 |   if (conn->state == BS_OPENCONFIRM)\n 3471 |   { 3477 | \n 3478 | /**\n 3479 |  * bgp_rx_packet - handle a received packet\n 3480 |  * @conn: BGP connection\n 3481 |  * @pkt: start of the packet\n 3482 |  * @len: packet size\n 3483 |  *\n 3484 |  * bgp_rx_packet() takes a newly received packet and calls the corresponding\n 3485 |  * packet handler according to the packet type.\n 3486 |  */\n 3487 | static void\n 3488 | bgp_rx_packet(struct bgp_conn *conn, byte *pkt, uint len)\n 3489 | {\n 3490 |   byte type = pkt[18];\n 3491 | \n 3492 |   DBG(\"BGP: Got packet %02x (%d bytes)\\n\", type, len);\n 3493 | PKT_NOTIFICATION:\treturn bgp_rx_notification(conn, pkt, len);\n 3504 |   case PKT_KEEPALIVE:\t\treturn bgp_rx_keepalive(conn);\n 3505 |   case PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH:\treturn bgp_rx_route_refresh(conn, @size: amount of data received\n 3514 |  *\n 3515 |  * bgp_rx() is called by the socket layer whenever new data arrive from\n 3516 |  * the underlying TCP connection. It assembles the data fragments to packets,\n 3517 |  * checks their headers and framing and passes complete packets to\n 3518 |  * bgp_rx_packet().\n 3519 |  */\n 3520 | int\n 3521 | bgp_rx(sock *sk, uint size)\n 3522 | {\n 3523 |   struct bgp_conn *conn = sk->data;\n 3524 | \n 3525 |   byte *pkt_start = sk->rbuf;\n 3526 |   byte *end = pkt_start + size;\n 3527 |   uint i, len;\n 3528 | \n 3529 |   DBG(\"BGP: RX hook: Got %d bytes\\n\", size);\n 3530 |   while (end >= pkt_start + BGP_HEADER_LENGTH)\n 3531 |     {\n 3532 |       if ((conn->state == BS_CLOSE) || (conn->sk != sk))\n 3533 | \treturn 0;\n 3534 |       for(i=0; i<16; i++)\n 3535 | \tif (pkt_start[i] != 0xff)\n 3536 | \t  {\n 3537 | \t    bgp_error(conn, 1, 1, NULL, 0);\n 3538 | \t    break;\n 3539 | \t  }\n 3540 |       len = get_u16(pkt_start+16);\n 3541 |       if ((len < BGP_HEADER_LENGTH) || (len > bgp_max_packet_length(conn)))\n 3542 | \t{\n 3543 | \t  bgp_error(conn, 1, 2, pkt_start+16, 2);\n 3544 | \t  break;\n 3545 | \t}\n 3546 |       if (end < pkt_start + len)\n 3547 | \tbreak;\n 3548 |       bgp_rx_packet(conn, pkt_start, len);\n 3549 |       pkt_start += len;\n 3550 |     }\n 3551 |   if (pkt_start != sk->rbuf)\n 3552 |     {\n 3553 |       memmove(sk->rbuf, pkt_start, end - pkt_start);\n 3554 |       sk->rpos = sk->rbuf + (end - pkt_start);\n 3555 |     }\n 3556 |   return 0;\n 3557 | }\n",

announced in multiprotocol capability */\n  205 |   return &dummy_af_caps;\n  206 | }\n  207 | \n  208 | static struct bgp_af_caps *\n  209 | bgp_get_af_caps(struct bgp_caps **pcaps, u32 afi)\n  210 | With max 12 channels that is\n  340 |    * 274. We are limited just by buffer size (4096, minus header), as we support\n  341 |    * extended optional parameres. Therefore, we have enough space for expansion.\n  342 |    */\n  343 | \n  344 |   WALK_AF_CAPS(caps, ac)\n  345 |     if (ac->ready)\n  346 |     {\n  347 |       *buf++ = 1;\t\t/* Capability 1: Multiprotocol extensions */\n  348 |       *buf++ = 4;\t\t/* Capability data length */\n  349 |       put_af4(buf, ac->afi);\n  350 |       buf += 4;\n  351 |     }\n  352 | \n  353 |   if |   if (caps->any_ext_next_hop)\n  360 |   {\n  361 |     *buf++ = 5;\t\t\t/* Capability 5: Support for extended next hop */\n  362 |     *buf++ = 0;\t\t\t/* Capability data length, will be fixed later */\n  363 |     data = buf;\n  364 | \n  365 |     WALK_AF_CAPS(caps, ac)\n  366 |       if (ac->ext_next_hop)\n  367 |       {\n  368 | \tput_af4(buf, ac->afi);\n  369 | \tput_u16(buf+4, BGP_AFI_IPV6);\n  370 | \tbuf += 6;\n  371 |       }\n  372 | \n  373 |     data[-1] = buf - data;\n  374 |   }\n  375 | \n  376 |   if (caps->ext_messages)\n  377 |   {\n  378 |     *buf++ = 6;\t\t\t/* Capability 6: Support for extended messages */\n  379 |     *buf++ = 0;\t\t\t/* Capability data length */\n  380 |   }\n  381 | \n  382 |   if (caps->role != BGP_ROLE_UNDEFINED)\n  383 |   {\n  384 |     *buf++ = 9;\t\t\t/* Capability 9: Announce chosen BGP role */\n  385 |     *buf++ = 1;\t\t\t/* Capability data length */\n  386 |     *buf++ = caps->role;\n  387 |   }\n  388 | \n  389 |   if (caps->gr_aware)\n  390 |   {\n  391 |     *buf++ = 64;\t\t/* Capability 64: Support for graceful restart */\n  392 |     *buf++ = 0;\t\t\t/* Capability data length, will be fixed later */\n  393 |     data = buf;\n  394 | \n  395 |     put_u16(buf, caps->gr_time);\n  396 |     buf[0] |= caps->gr_flags;\n  397 |     buf += 2;\n  398 | \n  399 |     WALK_AF_CAPS(caps, ac)\n  400 |       if (ac->gr_able)\n  401 |       {\n  402 | \tput_af3(buf, ac->afi);\n  403 | \tbuf[3] = ac->gr_af_flags;\n  404 | \tbuf += 4;\n  405 |       }\n  406 | \n  407 |     data[-1] = buf - data;\n  408 |   }\n  409 | \n  410 |   if (caps->as4_support)\n  411 |   {\n  412 |     *buf++ = 65;\t\t/* Capability 65: Support for 4-octet AS number */\n  413 |     *buf++ = 4;\t\t\t/* Capability data length */\n  414 |     put_u32(buf, p->public_as);\n  415 |     buf += 4;\n  416 |   }\n  417 | \n  418 |   if (caps->any_add_path)\n  419 |   {\n  420 |     *buf++ = 69;\t\t/* Capability 69: Support for ADD-PATH */\n  421 |     *buf++ = 0;\t\t\t/* Capability data length, will be fixed later */\n  422 |     data = buf;\n  423 | \n  424 |     WALK_AF_CAPS(caps, ac)\n  425 |       if (ac->add_path)\n  426 |       {\n  427 | \tput_af3(buf, ac->afi);\n  428 | \tbuf[3] = ac->add_path;\n  429 | \tbuf += 4;\n  430 |       }\n  431 | \n  432 |     data[-1] = buf - data;\n  433 |   }\n  434 | \n  435 |   if (caps->enhanced_refresh)\n  436 |   {\n  437 |     *buf++ = 70;\t\t/* Capability 70: Support for enhanced route refresh */\n  438 |     *buf++ = 0;\t\t\t/* Capability data length */\n  439 |   }\n  440 | \n  441 |   if (caps->llgr_aware)\n  442 |   {\n  443 |     *buf++ = 71;\t\t/* Capability 71: Support for long-lived graceful restart */\n  444 |     *buf++ = 0;\t\t\t/* Capability data length, will be fixed later */\n  445 |     data = buf;\n  446 | \n  447 |     WALK_AF_CAPS(caps, ac)\n  448 |       if (ac->llgr_able)\n  449 |       {\n  450 | \tput_af3(buf, ac->afi);\n  451 | \tbuf[3] = 460 |   {\n  461 |     *buf++ = 73;                /* Capability 73: Hostname */\n  462 |     *buf++ = 0;\t\t\t/* Capability data length */\n  463 |     data = buf;\n  464 | \n  465 |     /* Hostname */\n  466 |     size_t length = strlen(caps->hostname);\n  467 |     *buf++ = length;\n  468 |     memcpy(buf, caps->hostname, length);\n  469 |     buf += length;\n  470 | \n  471 |     /* Domain, not implemented */\n  472 |     *buf++ = 0;\n  473 | \n  474 |     data[-1] = buf - data;\n  475 |   }\n  476 | \n  477 |   caps->length = buf - buf_head;\n  478 | \n  479 |   return buf;\n  480 | }\n  481 | \n  482 | static int\n  483 | bgp_read_capabilities(struct bgp_conn *conn, byte *pos, int len)\n  484 | {\n  485 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n  486 |   struct bgp_caps *caps;\n  487 |   struct bgp_af_caps *ac;\n  488 |   uint err_subcode = 0;\n  489 |   int i, cl;\n  490 |   u32 af;\n  491 | \n  492 |   if (!conn->remote_caps)\n  493 |     caps = bgp_alloc_capabilities(p, 1);\n  494 |   else\n  495 |   {\n  496 |     caps = conn->remote_caps;\n  497 |     conn->remote_caps = NULL;\n  498 |   }\n  499 | \n  500 |   caps->length += len;\n  501 | \n  502 |   while (len > 0)\n  503 |   {\n  504 |     if (len < 2 || len < (2 + pos[1]))\n  505 |       goto err;\n  506 | \n  507 |     /* Capability length */\n  508 |     cl = pos[1];\n  509 | \n  510 |     /* Capability type */\n  511 |     switch (pos[0])\n  512 |     {\n  513 |     case  1: /* Multiprotocol capability, RFC 4760 */\n  514 |       if (cl != 4)\n  515 | \tgoto err;\n  516 | \n  517 |       af = get_af4(pos+2);\n  518 |       ac = bgp_get_af_caps(&caps, af);\n  519 |       ac->ready = 1;\n  520 |       break;\n  521 | \n  522 |     case  2: /* Route refresh capability, RFC 2918 */\n  523 |       if (cl != 0)\n  524 | \tgoto err;\n  525 | \n  526 |       caps->route_refresh = 1;\n  527 |       break;\n  528 | \n  529 |     case  5: /* Extended next hop encoding capability, RFC 8950 */\n  530 |       if (cl % 6)\n  531 | \tgoto err;\n  532 | \n  533 |       for (i = 0; i < cl; i += 6)\n  534 |       {\n  535 | \t/* Specified only for IPv4 prefixes with IPv6 next hops */\n  536 | \tif ((get_u16(pos+2+i+0) != BGP_AFI_IPV4) ||\n  537 | \t    (get_u16(pos+2+i+4) != BGP_AFI_IPV6))\n  538 | \t  continue;\n  539 | \n  540 | \taf = get_af4(pos+2+i);\n  541 | \tac = bgp_get_af_caps(&caps, af);\n  542 | \tac->ext_next_hop = 1;\n  543 |       }\n  544 |       break;\n  545 | \n  546 |     case  6: /* Extended message length capability, RFC 8654 */\n  547 |       if (cl != 0)\n  548 | \tgoto err;\n  549 | \n  550 |       caps->ext_messages = 1;\n  551 |       break;\n  552 | \n  553 |     case  9: /* BGP role capability, RFC 9234 */\n  554 |       if (cl != 1)\n  555 |         goto err;\n  556 | \n  557 |       /* Reserved value */\n  558 |       if (pos[2] == BGP_ROLE_UNDEFINED)\n  559 |       { err_subcode = 11; goto err; }\n  560 | \n  561 |       /* Multiple inconsistent values */\n  562 |       if ((caps->role != BGP_ROLE_UNDEFINED) && (caps->role != pos[2]))\n  563 |       { err_subcode = 11; goto err; }\n  564 | \n  565 |       caps->role = pos[2];\n  566 |       break;\n  567 | \n  568 |     case 64: /* Graceful restart capability, RFC 4724 */\n  569 |       if (cl % 4 != 2)\n  570 | \tgoto err;\n  571 | \n  572 |       /* Only the last instance is valid */\n  573 |       WALK_AF_CAPS(caps, ac)\n  574 |       {\n  575 | \tac->gr_able = 0;\n  576 | \tac->gr_af_flags = 0;\n  577 |       }\n  578 | \n  579 |       caps->gr_aware = 1;\n  580 |       caps->gr_flags = pos[2] & 0xf0;\n  581 |       caps->gr_time = get_u16(pos + 2) & 0x0fff;\n  582 | \n  583 |       for (i = 2; i < cl; i += 4)\n  584 |       {\n  585 | \taf = get_af3(pos+2+i);\n  586 | \tac = bgp_get_af_caps(&caps, af);\n  587 | \tac->gr_able = 1;\n  588 | \tac->gr_af_flags = pos[2+i+3];\n  589 |       }\n  590 |       break;\n  591 | \n  592 |     case 65: /* AS4 capability, RFC 6793 */\n  593 |       if (cl != 4)\n  594 | \tgoto err;\n  595 | \n  596 |       caps->as4_support = 1;\n  597 |       caps->as4_number = get_u32(pos + 2);\n  598 |       break;\n  599 | \n  600 |     case 69: /* ADD-PATH capability, RFC 7911 */\n  601 |       if (cl % 4)\n  602 | \tgoto err;\n  603 | \n  604 |       for (i = 0; i < cl; i += 4)\n  605 |       {\n  606 | \tbyte val = pos[2+i+3];\n  607 | \tif (!val || (val > BGP_ADD_PATH_FULL))\n  608 | \t{\n  609 | \t  log(L_WARN \"%s: Got ADD-PATH capability with unknown value %u, ignoring\",\n  610 | \t      p->p.name, val);\n  611 | \t  break;\n  612 | \t}\n  613 |       }\n  614 | \n  615 |       for (i = 0; i < cl; i += 4)\n  616 |       {\n  617 | \taf = get_af3(pos+2+i);\n  618 | \tac = bgp_get_af_caps(&caps, af);\n  619 | \tac->add_path = pos[2+i+3];\n  620 |       }\n  621 |       break;\n  622 | \n  623 |     case 70: /* Enhanced route refresh capability, RFC 7313 */\n  624 |       if (cl != 0)\n  625 | \tgoto err;\n  626 | \n  627 |       caps->enhanced_refresh = 1;\n  628 |       break;\n  629 | \n  630 |     case 71: /* Long lived graceful restart capability, RFC 9494 */\n  631 |       if (cl % 7)\n  632 | \tgoto err;\n  633 | \n  634 |       /* Presumably, only the last instance is valid */\n  635 |       WALK_AF_CAPS(caps, ac)\n  636 |       {\n  637 | \tac->llgr_able = 0;\n  638 | \tac->llgr_flags = 0;\n  639 | \tac->llgr_time = 0;\n  640 |       }\n  641 | \n  642 |       caps->llgr_aware = 1;\n  643 | \n  644 |       for (i = 0; i < cl; i += 7)\n  645 |       {\n  646 | \taf = get_af3(pos+2+i);\n  647 | \tac = bgp_get_af_caps(&caps, af);\n  648 | \tac->llgr_able = 1;\n  649 | \tac->llgr_flags = pos[2+i+3];\n  650 | \tac->llgr_time = get_u24(pos + 2+i+4);\n  651 |       }\n  652 |       break;\n  653 | \n  654 |     case 73: /* Hostname, RFC draft */\n  655 |       if ((cl < 2) || (cl < 2 + pos[2]))\n  656 |         goto err;\n  657 | \n  658 |       int length = pos[2];\n  659 |       char *hostname = mb_allocz(p->p.pool, length+1);\n  660 |       memcpy(hostname, pos + 3, length);\n  661 |       hostname[length] = 0;\n  662 | \n  663 |       for (i = 0; i < length; i++)\n  664 |         if (hostname[i] < ' ')\n  665 |           hostname[i] = ' ';\n  666 | \n  667 |       caps->hostname = hostname;\n  668 | \n  669 |       /* We can safely ignore all other capabilities */\n  670 |     }\n  671 | \n  672 |     ADVANCE(pos, len, 2 + cl);\n  673 |   }\n  674 | \n  675 |   /* The LLGR capability must be advertised together with the GR capability,\n  676 |      otherwise it must be disregarded */\n  677 |   if (!caps->gr_aware && caps->llgr_aware)\n  678 |   {\n  679 |     caps->llgr_aware = 0;\n  680 |     WALK_AF_CAPS(caps, ac)\n  681 |     {\n  682 |       ac->llgr_able = 0;\n  683 |       ac->llgr_flags = 0;\n  684 |       ac->llgr_time = 0;\n  685 |     }\n  686 |   }\n  687 | \n  688 |   conn->remote_caps = caps;\n  689 |   return 0;\n  690 | \n  691 | err:\n  692 |   mb_free(caps);\n  693 |   bgp_error(conn, 2, err_subcode, NULL, 0);\n  694 |   return -1;\n  695 | }\n  696 | \n  697 | int\n  698 | bgp_check_capabilities(struct bgp_conn *conn)\n  699 | {\n  700 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n  701 |   struct bgp_caps *local = conn->local_caps;\n  702 |   struct bgp_caps *remote = conn->remote_caps;\n  703 |   struct bgp_channel *c;\n  704 |   int count = 0;\n  705 | \n  706 |   /* This is partially overlapping with bgp_conn_enter_established_state(),\n  707 |      but we need to run this just after we receive OPEN message */\n  708 | \n  709 |   if (p->cf->require_refresh && !remote->route_refresh)\n  710 |     return 0;\n  711 | \n  712 |   if (p->cf->require_enhanced_refresh && !remote->enhanced_refresh)\n  713 |     return 0;\n  714 | \n  715 |   if (p->cf->require_as4 && !remote->as4_support)\n  716 |     return 0;\n  717 | \n  718 |   if (p->cf->require_extended_messages && !remote->ext_messages)\n  719 |     return 0;\n  720 | \n  721 |   if (p->cf->require_hostname && !remote->hostname)\n  722 |     return 0;\n  723 | \n  724 |   if (p->cf->require_gr && !remote->gr_aware)\n  725 |     return 0;\n  726 | \n  727 |   if (p->cf->require_llgr && !remote->llgr_aware)\n  728 |     return 0;\n  729 | \n  730 |   /* No check for require_roles, as it uses error code 2.11 instead of 2.7 */\n  731 | \n  732 |   BGP_WALK_CHANNELS(p, c)\n  733 |   {\n  734 |     const struct bgp_af_caps *loc = bgp_find_af_caps(local,  c->afi);\n  735 |     const struct bgp_af_caps *rem = bgp_find_af_caps(remote, c->afi);\n  736 | \n  737 |     /* Find out whether this channel will be active */\n  738 |     int active = loc->ready && rem->ready;\n  739 | \n  740 |     /* Mandatory must be active */\n  741 |     if (c->cf->mandatory && !active)\n  742 |       return 0;\n  743 | \n  744 |     if (active)\n  745 |     {\n  746 |       if (c->cf->require_ext_next_hop && !rem->ext_next_hop)\n  747 | \treturn 0;\n  748 | \n  749 |       if (c->cf->require_add_path && (loc->add_path & BGP_ADD_PATH_RX) && !(rem->add_path & BGP_ADD_PATH_TX))\n  750 | \treturn 0;\n  751 | \n  752 |       if (c->cf->require_add_path && (loc->add_path & BGP_ADD_PATH_TX) && !(rem->add_path & BGP_ADD_PATH_RX))\n  753 | \treturn 0;\n  754 | \n  755 |       count++;\n  756 |     }\n  757 |   }\n  758 | \n  759 |   /* We need at least one channel active */\n  760 |   if (!count)\n  761 |     return 0;\n  762 | \n  763 |   return 1;\n  764 | }\n  765 | \n  766 | static int\n  767 | bgp_read_options(struct bgp_conn *conn, byte *pos, uint len, uint rest)\n  768 | {\n  769 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n  770 |   int ext = 0;\n  771 | \n  772 |   /* Handle extended length, RFC 9072 */\n  773 |   if ((len > 0) && (rest > 0) && (pos[0] == 255))\n  774 |   {\n  775 |     if (rest < 3)\n  776 |       goto err;\n  777 | \n  778 |     /* Update pos/len to describe optional data */\n  779 |     len = get_u16(pos+1);\n  780 |     ext = 1;\n  781 |     pos += 3;\n  782 |     rest -= 3;\n  783 |   }\n  784 | \n  785 |   /* Verify that optional data fits into OPEN packet */\n  786 |   if (len > rest)\n  787 |     goto err;\n  788 | \n  789 |   /* Length of option parameter header */\n  790 |   uint hlen = ext ? 3 : 2;\n  791 | \n  792 |   while (len > 0)\n  793 |   {\n  794 |     if (len < hlen)\n  795 |       goto err;\n  796 | \n  797 |     uint otype = get_u8(pos);\n  798 |     uint olen = ext ? get_u16(pos+1) : get_u8(pos+1);\n  799 | \n  800 |     if (len < (hlen + olen))\n  801 |       goto err;\n  802 | \n  803 |     if (otype == 2)\n  804 |     {\n  805 |       /* BGP capabilities, RFC 5492 */\n  806 |       if (p->cf->capabilities)\n  807 | \tif (bgp_read_capabilities(conn, pos + hlen, olen) < 0)\n  808 | \t  return -1;\n  809 |     }\n  810 |     else\n  811 |     {\n  812 |       /* Unknown option */\n  813 |       bgp_error(conn, 2, 4, pos, hlen + olen);\n  814 |       return -1;\n  815 |     }\n  816 | \n  817 |     ADVANCE(pos, len, hlen + olen);\n  818 |   }\n  819 | \n  820 |   /* Prepare empty caps if no capability option was announced */\n  821 |   if (!conn->remote_caps)\n  822 |     conn->remote_caps = bgp_alloc_capabilities(p, 0);\n  823 | \n  824 |   return 0;\n  825 | \n  826 | err:\n  827 |   bgp_error(conn, 2, 0, NULL, 0);\n  828 |   return -1;\n  829 | }\n  830 | \n  831 | static byte *\n  832 | bgp_copy_open(struct bgp_proto *p, const byte *pkt, uint len)\n  833 | {\n  834 |   char *buf = mb_alloc(p->p.pool, len - BGP_HEADER_LENGTH);\n  835 |   memcpy(buf, pkt + BGP_HEADER_LENGTH, len - BGP_HEADER_LENGTH);\n  836 |   return buf;\n  837 | }\n  838 | \n  839 | static byte *\n  840 | bgp_create_open(struct bgp_conn *conn, byte *buf)\n  841 | {\n  842 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n  843 | \n  844 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Sending OPEN(ver=%d,as=%d,hold=%d,id=%08x)\",\n  845 | \t    BGP_VERSION, p->public_as, p->cf->hold_time, p->local_id);\n  846 | \n  847 |   buf[0] = BGP_VERSION;\n  848 |   put_u16(buf+1, (p->public_as < 0xFFFF) ? p->public_as : AS_TRANS);\n  849 |   put_u16(buf+3, p->cf->hold_time);\n  850 |   put_u32(buf+5, p->local_id);\n  851 | \n  852 |   if (p->cf->capabilities)\n  853 |   {\n  854 |     /* Prepare local_caps and write capabilities to buffer */\n  855 |     byte *pos = buf+12;\n  856 |     byte *end = bgp_write_capabilities(conn, pos);\n  857 |     uint len = end - pos;\n  858 | \n  859 |     if (len < 254)\n  860 |     {\n  861 |       buf[9] = len + 2;\t\t/* Optional parameters length */\n  862 |       buf[10] = 2;\t\t/* Option 2: Capability list */\n  863 |       buf[11] = len;\t\t/* Option data length */\n  864 |     }\n  865 |     else /* Extended length, RFC 9072 */\n  866 |     {\n  867 |       /* Move capabilities 4 B forward */\n  868 |       memmove(buf + 16, pos, len);\n  869 |       pos = buf + 16;\n  870 |       end = pos + len;\n  871 | \n  872 |       buf[9] = 255;\t\t/* Non-ext OP length, fake */\n  873 |       buf[10] = 255;\t\t/* Non-ext OP type, signals extended length */\n  874 |       put_u16(buf+11, len + 3);\t/* Extended optional parameters length */\n  875 |       buf[13] = 2;\t\t/* Option 2: Capability list */\n  876 |       put_u16(buf+14, len);\t/* Option extended data length */\n  877 |     }\n  878 | \n  879 |     return end;\n  880 |   }\n  881 |   else\n  882 |   {\n  883 |     buf[9] = 0;\t\t\t/* No optional parameters */\n  884 |     return buf + 10;\n  885 |   }\n  886 | \n  887 |   return buf;\n  888 | }\n  889 | \n  890 | static void\n  891 | bgp_rx_open(struct bgp_conn *conn, byte *pkt, uint len)\n  892 | {\n  893 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n  894 |   struct bgp_conn *other;\n  895 |   u32 asn, hold, id;\n  896 | \n  897 |   /* Check state */\n  898 |   if (conn->state != BS_OPENSENT)\n  899 |   { bgp_error(conn, 5, fsm_err_subcode[conn->state], NULL, 0); return; }\n  900 | \n  901 |   /* Check message length */\n  902 |   if (len < 29)\n  903 |   { bgp_error(conn, 1, 2, pkt+16, 2); return; }\n  904 | \n  905 |   if (pkt[19] != BGP_VERSION)\n  906 |   { u16 val = BGP_VERSION; bgp_error(conn, 2, 1, (byte *) &val, 2); return; }\n  907 | \n  908 |   asn = get_u16(pkt+20);\n  909 |   hold = get_u16(pkt+22);\n  910 |   id = get_u32(pkt+24);\n  911 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Got OPEN(as=%d,hold=%d,id=%R)\", asn, hold, id);\n  912 | \n  913 |   conn->remote_open_msg = bgp_copy_open(p, pkt, len);\n  914 |   conn->remote_open_length = len - BGP_HEADER_LENGTH;\n  915 | \n  916 |   if (bgp_read_options(conn, pkt+29, pkt[28], len-29) < 0)\n  917 |     return;\n  918 | \n  919 |   /* RFC 4271 4.2 - hold time must be either 0 or at least 3 */\n  920 |   if (hold > 0 && hold < 3)\n  921 |   { bgp_error(conn, 2, 6, pkt+22, 2); return; }\n  922 | \n  923 |   /* Compute effective hold and keepalive times */\n  924 |   uint hold_time = MIN(hold, p->cf->hold_time);\n  925 |   uint keepalive_time = p->cf->keepalive_time ?\n  926 |     (p->cf->keepalive_time * hold_time / p->cf->hold_time) :\n  927 |     hold_time / 3;\n  928 | \n  929 |   uint send_hold_time = (p->cf->send_hold_time >= 0) ?\n  930 |     (p->cf->send_hold_time * hold_time / p->cf->hold_time) :\n  931 |     2 * hold_time;\n  932 | \n  933 |   /* Keepalive time might be rounded down to zero */\n  934 |   if (hold_time && !keepalive_time)\n  935 |     keepalive_time = 1;\n  936 | \n  937 |   /* Check effective values against configured minimums */\n  938 |   if ((hold_time < p->cf->min_hold_time) ||\n  939 |       (keepalive_time < p->cf->min_keepalive_time))\n  940 |   { bgp_error(conn, 2, 6, pkt+22, 2); return; }\n  941 | \n  942 |   /* RFC 6286 2.2 - router ID is nonzero and AS-wide unique */\n  943 |   if (!id || (p->is_internal && id == p->local_id))\n  944 |   { bgp_error(conn, 2, 3, pkt+24, -4); return; }\n  945 | \n  946 |   /* RFC 5492 5 - check for required capabilities */\n  947 |   if (p->cf->capabilities && !bgp_check_capabilities(conn))\n  948 |   { bgp_error(conn, 2, 7, NULL, 0); return; }\n  949 | \n  950 |   struct bgp_caps *caps = conn->remote_caps;\n  951 | \n  952 |   if (caps->as4_support)\n  953 |   {\n  954 |     u32 as4 = caps->as4_number;\n  955 | \n  956 |     if ((as4 != asn) && (asn != AS_TRANS))\n  957 |       log(L_WARN \"%s: Peer advertised inconsistent AS numbers\", p->p.name);\n  958 | \n  959 |     /* When remote ASN is unspecified, it must be external one */\n  960 |     if (p->remote_as ? (as4 != p->remote_as) : (as4 == p->local_as))\n  961 |     { as4 = htonl(as4); bgp_error(conn, 2, 2, (byte *) &as4, 4); return; }\n  962 | \n  963 |     conn->received_as = as4;\n  964 |   }\n  965 |   else\n  966 |   {\n  967 |     if (p->remote_as ? Router with higher ID is dominant\n 1010 |      * 2. If both have the same ID, router with higher ASN is dominant [RFC6286]\n 1011 |      * 3. When both connections are in OpenConfirm state, one initiated by\n 1012 |      *    the dominant router is kept.\n 1013 |      *\n 1014 |      * The first line in the expression below evaluates whether the neighbor\n 1015 |      * is dominant, the second line whether the new connection was initiated\n 1016 |      * by the neighbor. If both are true (or both are false), we keep the new\n 1017 |      * connection, otherwise we keep the old one.\n 1018 |      */\n 1019 |     if (((p->local_id < id) || ((p->local_id == id) && (p->public_as < p->remote_as)))\n 1020 | \t== (conn == &p->incoming_conn))\n 1021 |     {\n 1022 |       /* Should close the other connection */\n 1023 |       BGP_TRACE(D_EVENTS, \"Connection collision, giving up the other connection\");\n 1024 |       bgp_error(other, 6, 7, NULL, 0);\n 1025 |       break;\n 1026 |     }\n 1027 |     /* Fall thru */\n 1028 |   case BS_ESTABLISHED:\n 1029 |     /* Should close this connection */\n 1030 |     BGP_TRACE(D_EVENTS, \"Connection collision, giving up this connection\");\n 1031 |     bgp_error(conn, 6, 7, NULL, 0);\n 1032 |     return;\n 1033 | \n 1034 |   default:\n 1035 |     bug(\"bgp_rx_open: Unknown state\");\n 1036 |   }\n 1037 | \n 1038 |   /* Update our local variables */\n 1039 |   conn->hold_time = hold_time;\n 1040 |   conn->keepalive_time = keepalive_time;\n 1041 |   conn->send_hold_time = send_hold_time;\n 1042 |   conn->as4_session = conn->local_caps->as4_support && caps->as4_support;\n 1043 |   conn->ext_messages = conn->local_caps->ext_messages && caps->ext_messages;\n 1044 |   p->remote_id = id;\n 1045 | \n 1046 |   DBG(\"BGP: Hold timer set to %d, keepalive to %d, AS to %d, ID to %x, AS4 session to %d\\n\",\n 1047 |       conn->hold_time, conn->keepalive_time, p->remote_as, p->remote_id, conn->as4_session);\n 1048 | \n 1049 |   bgp_schedule_packet(conn, NULL, PKT_KEEPALIVE);\n 1050 | 1055 | \n 1056 | /*\n 1057 |  *\tNext hop handling\n 1058 |  */\n 1059 | \n 1060 | #define REPORT(msg, args...) \\\n 1061 |   ({ log(L_REMOTE \"%s: \" msg, s->proto->p.name, ## args); })\n 1062 | \n 1063 | #define DISCARD(msg, args...) \\\n 1064 |   ({ REPORT(msg, ## args); return; })\n 1065 | \n 1066 | #define WITHDRAW(msg, args...) \\\n 1067 |   ({ REPORT(msg, ## args); s->err_withdraw = 1; bgp_parse_state *s, rta *a, ip_addr gw, ip_addr ll)\n 1081 | {\n 1082 |   struct bgp_proto *p = s->proto;\n 1083 |   struct bgp_channel *c = s->channel;\n 1084 | \n 1085 |   if (c->cf->gw_mode == | bgp_decode_next_hop_vpn(struct bgp_parse_state *s, byte *data, uint len, rta *a)\n 1473 | {\n 1474 |   struct bgp_channel *c = s->channel;\n 1475 |   struct adata *ad = lp_alloc_adata(s->pool, void\n 1542 | bgp_update_next_hop_none(struct bgp_export_state *s, eattr *a, ea_list **to)\n 1543 | {\n 1544 |   /* NEXT_HOP shall not pass */\n 1545 |   if (a)\n 1546 |     bgp_unset_attr(to, u32 path_id, rta *a0)\n 1556 | {\n 1557 |   if (path_id != s->last_id)\n 1558 |   {\n 1559 |     s->last_src = rt_get_source(&s->proto->p, path_id);\n 1560 |     s->last_id = path_id;\n 1561 | \n 1562 |     rta_free(s->cached_rta);\n 1563 |     s->cached_rta = NULL;\n 1564 |   }\n 1565 | \n 1566 |   if (!a0)\n 1567 |   {\n 1568 |     /* Route update was changed to withdraw */\n 1569 |     if bgp_encode_mpls_labels(struct bgp_write_state *s UNUSED, const adata *mpls, byte **pos, uint *size, byte *pxlen)\n 1594 | {\n 1595 |   const u32 dummy = 0;\n 1596 |   const u32 *labels = mpls ? (const u32 *) mpls->data : &dummy;\n 1597 |   uint lnum = mpls ? (mpls->length / 4) : 1;\n 1598 | \n 1599 |   for (uint i = 0; i < lnum; i++)\n 1600 |   {\n 1601 |     put_u24(*pos, labels[i] << 4);\n 1602 |     ADVANCE(*pos, *size, 3);\n 1603 |   }\n 1604 | \n 1605 |   /* Add bottom-of-stack flag */\n 1606 |   (*pos)[-1] |= BGP_MPLS_BOS;\n 1607 | \n 1608 |   *pxlen += 24 * lnum;\n 1609 | }\n 1610 | \n 1611 | static void\n 1612 | bgp_decode_mpls_labels(struct bgp_parse_state *s, byte **pos, uint *len, uint *pxlen, rta *a)\n 1613 | {\n 1614 |   u32 labels[BGP_MPLS_MAX], label;\n 1615 |   uint lnum = 0;\n 1616 | \n 1617 |   do {\n 1618 |     if (*pxlen < 24)\n 1619 |       bgp_parse_error(s, 1);\n 1620 | \n 1621 |     label = get_u24(*pos);\n 1622 |     labels[lnum++] = label >> 4;\n 1623 |     ADVANCE(*pos, *len, 3);\n 1624 |     *pxlen -= 24;\n 1625 | \n 1626 |     /* RFC 8277 2.4 - withdraw does not have variable-size MPLS stack but\n 1627 |        fixed-size 24-bit Compatibility field, which MUST be ignored */\n 1628 |     if (!s->reach_nlri_step)\n 1629 |       return;\n 1630 |   }\n 1631 |   while (!(label & BGP_MPLS_BOS));\n 1632 | \n 1633 |   if (!a)\n 1634 |     return;\n 1635 | \n 1636 |   /* Attach MPLS attribute unless we already have one */\n 1637 |   if (!s->mpls_labels)\n 1638 |   {\n 1639 |     s->mpls_labels = lp_alloc_adata(s->pool, 4*BGP_MPLS_MAX);\n 1640 |     bgp_set_attr_ptr(&(a->eattrs), s->pool, BA_MPLS_LABEL_STACK, 0, s->mpls_labels);\n 1641 |   }\n 1642 | \n 1643 |   /* Overwrite data in the attribute */\n 1644 |   s->mpls_labels->length = 4*lnum;\n 1645 |   memcpy(s->mpls_labels->data, labels, 4*lnum);\n 1646 | \n 1647 |   /* Update next hop entry in rta */\n 1648 |   bgp_apply_mpls_labels(s, a, labels, lnum);\n 1649 | \n 1650 |   /* Attributes bgp_encode_nlri_ip4(struct bgp_write_state *s, struct bgp_bucket *buck, byte *buf, uint size)\n 1659 | {\n 1660 |   byte *pos = buf;\n 1661 | \n 1662 |   while (!EMPTY_LIST(buck->prefixes) && (size >=   bgp_create_mp_unreach(&s, buck, buf, end);\n 2635 | \n 2636 |     goto done;\n 2637 |   }\n 2638 | \n 2639 |   /* Try reachable buckets */\n 2640 |   if (!EMPTY_LIST(c->bucket_queue))\n 2641 |   {\n 2642 |     buck = HEAD(c->bucket_queue);\n 2643 | \n 2644 |     /* Cleanup empty buckets */\n 2645 |     if (EMPTY_LIST(buck->prefixes))\n 2646 |     {\n 2647 |       bgp_free_bucket(c, buck);\n 2648 |       lp_restore(tmp_linpool, &tmpp);\n 2649 |       goto again;\n 2650 |     }\n 2651 | \n 2652 |     res = !s.mp_reach ?\n 2653 |       bgp_create_ip_reach(&s, buck, buf, end):\n 2654 |       bgp_create_mp_reach(&s, buck, buf, end);\n 2655 | \n 2656 |     if (EMPTY_LIST(buck->prefixes))\n 2657 |       bgp_free_bucket(c, buck);\n 2658 |     else\n 2659 |       bgp_defer_bucket(c, buck);\n 2660 | \n 2661 |     if (!res)\n 2662 |     {\n 2663 |       lp_restore(tmp_linpool, &tmpp);\n 2664 |       goto again;\n 2665 |     }\n 2666 | \n 2667 |     goto done;\n 2668 |   }\n 2669 | \n 2670 |   /* No more prefixes to send */\n 2671 |   return NULL;\n 2672 | \n 2673 | done:\n 2674 |   BGP_TRACE_RL(&rl_snd_update, D_PACKETS, \"Sending UPDATE\");\n 2675 |   p->stats.tx_updates++;\n 2676 |   lp_restore(tmp_linpool, &tmpp);\n 2677 | \n 2678 |   return res;\n 2679 | }\n 2680 | \n 2681 | static byte *\n 2682 | bgp_create_ip_end_mark(struct bgp_channel *c UNUSED, byte *buf)\n 2683 | {\n 2684 |   /* Empty update packet */\n 2685 |   put_u32(buf, 0);\n 2686 | \n 2687 |   return buf+4;\n 2688 | }\n 2689 | \n 2690 | static byte *\n 2691 | bgp_create_mp_end_mark(struct bgp_channel *c, byte BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Sending END-OF-RIB\");\n 2719 |   p->stats.tx_updates++;\n 2720 | \n 2721 |   return bgp_create_end_mark_(c, buf);\n 2722 | }\n 2723 | \n 2724 | static inline void\n 2725 | bgp_rx_end_mark(struct bgp_parse_state *s, u32 afi)\n 2726 | {\n 2727 |   struct bgp_proto *p = s->proto;\n 2728 |   struct bgp_channel *c = bgp_get_channel(p, afi);\n 2729 | \n 2730 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Got END-OF-RIB\");\n 2731 | \n 2732 |   if (!c)\n 2733 |     DISCARD(BAD_AFI, BGP_AFI(afi), BGP_SAFI(afi));\n 2734 | \n 2735 |   if (c->load_state == BFS_LOADING)\n 2736 |     c->load_state = BFS_NONE;\n 2737 | \n 2738 |   if (p->p.gr_recovery)\n 2739 |     channel_graceful_restart_unlock(&c->c);\n 2740 | \n 2741 |   if (c->gr_active)\n 2742 |     bgp_graceful_restart_done(c);\n 2743 | }\n 2744 | \n 2745 | static inline void\n 2746 | bgp_decode_nlri(struct bgp_parse_state *s, u32 afi, byte *nlri, uint len, ea_list *ea, byte *nh, uint nh_len)\n 2747 | {\n 2748 |   struct bgp_channel *c = bgp_get_channel(s->proto, afi);\n 2749 |   rta *a = NULL;\n 2750 | \n 2751 |   if (!c)\n 2752 |     DISCARD(BAD_AFI, BGP_AFI(afi), BGP_SAFI(afi));\n 2753 | \n 2754 |   s->channel = c;\n 2755 |   s->add_path = c->add_path_rx;\n 2756 |   s->mpls = c->desc->mpls;\n 2757 | \n 2758 |   s->last_id = 0;\n 2759 |   s->last_src = s->proto->p.main_source;\n 2760 | \n 2761 |   /*\n 2762 |    * IPv4 BGP and MP-BGP may be used together in one update, therefore we do not\n 2763 |    * add BA_NEXT_HOP in bgp_decode_attrs(), but we add it here independently for\n 2764 |    * IPv4 BGP and MP-BGP. We undo the attribute (and possibly others attached by\n 2765 |    * decode_next_hop hooks) by restoring a->eattrs afterwards.\n 2766 |    */\n 2767 | \n 2768 |   if (ea)\n 2769 |   {\n 2770 |     a = allocz(RTA_MAX_SIZE);\n 2771 | \n 2772 |     a->source = RTS_BGP;\n 2773 |     a->scope = SCOPE_UNIVERSE;\n 2774 |     a->from = s->proto->remote_ip;\n 2775 |     a->eattrs = ea;\n 2776 |     a->pref = c->c.preference;\n 2777 | \n 2778 |     c->desc->decode_next_hop(s, nh, nh_len, a);\n 2779 |     bgp_finish_attrs(s, a);\n 2780 | \n 2781 |     /* Handle withdraw during next hop decoding */\n 2782 |     if (s->err_withdraw)\n 2783 |       a = NULL;\n 2784 |   }\n 2785 | \n 2786 |   c->desc->decode_nlri(s, nlri, len, a);\n 2787 | \n 2788 |   rta_free(s->cached_rta);\n 2789 |   s->cached_rta = NULL;\n 2790 | }\n 2791 | \n 2792 | static void\n 2793 | bgp_rx_update(struct bgp_conn *conn, byte *pkt, uint len)\n 2794 | {\n 2795 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n 2796 |   ea_list *ea = NULL;\n 2797 | \n 2798 |   BGP_TRACE_RL(&rl_rcv_update, D_PACKETS, \"Got UPDATE\");\n 2799 |   p->last_rx_update = current_time();\n 2800 |   p->stats.rx_updates++;\n 2801 | \n 2802 |   /* Workaround for some BGP implementations that skip initial KEEPALIVE */\n 2803 |   if (conn->state == BS_OPENCONFIRM)\n 2804 |     bgp_conn_enter_established_state(conn);\n 2805 | \n 2806 |   if (conn->state != BS_ESTABLISHED)\n 2807 |   { bgp_error(conn, 5, fsm_err_subcode[conn->state], NULL, 0); goto done; }\n 2870 | \n 2871 |   /* Check for MP End-of-RIB marker */\n 2872 |   if ((s.attr_len < 8) && !s.ip_unreach_len && !s.ip_reach_len &&\n 2873 |       !s.mp_reach_len && !s.mp_unreach_len && s.mp_unreach_af)\n 2874 |   { bgp_rx_end_mark(&s, s.mp_unreach_af); Returns 0 for framing errors.\n 2905 |    */\n 2906 |   if (len < 23)\n 2907 |     return 0;\n 2908 | \n 2909 |   /* Assume there is no withrawn NLRI, read lengths and move to attribute list */\n 2910 |   uint wlen = get_u16(pos + 19);\n 2911 |   uint alen = get_u16(pos + 21);\n 2912 |   ADVANCE(pos, len, 23);\n 2913 | \n 2914 |   /* Either non-zero withdrawn NLRI, non-zero reachable NLRI, or IPv4 End-of-RIB */\n 2915 |   if ((wlen != 0) || (alen < len) || !alen)\n 2916 |     return BGP_AF_IPV4;\n 2917 | \n 2918 |   if (alen > len)\n 2919 |     return 0;\n 2920 | \n 2921 |   /* Process attribute list (alen == len) */\n 2922 |   while (len)\n 2923 |   {\n 2924 |     if (len < 2)\n 2925 |       return 0;\n 2926 | \n 2927 |     uint flags = pos[0];\n 2928 |     uint code = pos[1];\n 2929 |     ADVANCE(pos, len, 2);\n 2930 | \n 2931 |     uint ll = !(flags & BAF_EXT_LEN) ? 1 : 2;\n 2932 |     if (len < ll)\n 2933 |       return 0;\n 2934 | \n 2935 |     /* Read attribute length and move to attribute body */\n 2936 |     alen = (ll == 1) ? get_u8(pos) : get_u16(pos);\n 2937 |     ADVANCE(pos, len, ll);\n 2938 | \n 2939 |     if (len < alen)\n 2940 |       return 0;\n 2941 | \n 2942 |     /* Found MP NLRI */\n 2943 |     if ((code == BA_MP_REACH_NLRI) || (code == BA_MP_UNREACH_NLRI))\n 2944 |     {\n 2945 |       if (alen < 3)\n 2946 | \treturn 0;\n 2947 | \n 2948 | NLRI, but there are some attributes -> error */\n 2956 |   return 0;\n 2957 | }\n 2958 | \n 2959 | \n 2960 | /*\n 2961 |  *\tROUTE-REFRESH\n 2962 |  */\n 2963 | \n 2964 | static inline byte *\n 2965 | bgp_create_route_refresh(struct bgp_channel *c, byte *buf)\n 2966 | {\n 2967 |   struct bgp_proto *p = (void *) c->c.proto;\n 2968 | \n 2969 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Sending ROUTE-REFRESH\");\n 2970 | \n 2971 |   /* Original route refresh request, RFC 2918 */\n 2972 |   put_af4(buf, c->afi);\n 2973 |   buf[2] = BGP_RR_REQUEST;\n 2974 | \n 2975 |   return buf+4;\n 2976 | }\n 2977 | \n 2978 | static inline byte *\n 2979 | bgp_create_begin_refresh(struct bgp_channel *c, byte *buf)\n 2980 | {\n 2981 |   struct bgp_proto *p = (void *) c->c.proto;\n 2982 | \n 2983 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, return buf+4;\n 2990 | }\n 2991 | \n 2992 | static inline byte *\n 2993 | bgp_create_end_refresh(struct bgp_channel *c, byte *buf)\n 2994 | {\n 2995 |   struct bgp_proto *p = (void *) c->c.proto;\n 2996 | \n 2997 |   BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Sending END-OF-RR\");\n 2998 | \n 2999 |   /* Demarcation of ending of route refresh (EoRR), RFC 7313 */\n 3000 |   put_af4(buf, c->afi);\n 3001 |   buf[2] = bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n 3010 | \n 3011 |   if (conn->state != BS_ESTABLISHED)\n 3012 |   { bgp_error(conn, 5, fsm_err_subcode[conn->state], NULL, 0); return; }\n 3013 | \n 3014 |   if (!conn->local_caps->route_refresh)\n 3015 |   { bgp_error(conn, 1, 3, pkt+18, 1); return; }\n 3016 | \n 3017 |   if (len < (BGP_HEADER_LENGTH + 4))\n 3018 |   { bgp_error(conn, 1, 2, pkt+16, 2); |     return;\n 3029 |   }\n 3030 | \n 3031 |   /* RFC 7313 redefined reserved field as RR message subtype */\n 3032 |   uint subtype = p->enhanced_refresh ? pkt[21] : BGP_RR_REQUEST;\n 3033 | \n 3034 |   switch (subtype)\n 3035 |   {\n 3036 |   case BGP_RR_REQUEST:\n 3037 |     BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Got ROUTE-REFRESH\");\n 3038 |     channel_request_feeding(&c->c);\n 3039 |     break;\n 3040 | \n 3041 |   case BGP_RR_BEGIN:\n 3042 |     BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Got BEGIN-OF-RR\");\n 3043 |     bgp_refresh_begin(c);\n 3044 |     break;\n 3045 | \n 3046 |   case BGP_RR_END:\n 3047 | %u, ignoring\",\n 3053 | \tp->p.name, subtype);\n 3054 |     break;\n 3055 |   }\n 3056 | }\n 3057 | \n 3058 | static inline struct bgp_channel *\n 3059 | bgp_get_channel_to_send(struct bgp_proto *p, struct bgp_conn *conn)\n 3060 | {\n 3061 |   uint i = conn->last_channel;\n 3062 | \n 3063 |   /* Try the last channel, but at most several times */\n 3064 |   if ((conn->channels_to_send & (1 << i)) &&\n 3065 |       (conn->last_channel_count < 16))\n 3066 |     goto found;\n 3067 | \n 3068 |   /* Find channel with non-zero channels_to_send */\n 3069 |   do\n 3070 |   {\n 3071 |     i++;\n 3072 |     if (i >= p->channel_count)\n 3073 |       i = 0;\n 3074 |   }\n 3075 |   while (! (conn->channels_to_send & (1 << i)));\n 3076 | \n 3077 |   /* Use that channel */\n 3078 |   conn->last_channel = i;\n 3079 |   conn->last_channel_count = 0;\n 3080 | \n 3081 | found:\n 3082 |   conn->last_channel_count++;\n 3083 |   return p->channel_map[i];\n 3084 | }\n 3085 | \n 3086 | static inline int\n 3087 | bgp_send(struct bgp_conn *conn, uint type, uint len)\n 3088 | {\n 3089 |   sock *sk = conn->sk;\n 3090 |   byte *buf = sk->tbuf;\n 3091 | \n 3092 |   conn->bgp->stats.tx_messages++;\n 3093 |   conn->bgp->stats.tx_bytes += len;\n 3094 | \n 3095 |   memset(buf, 0xff, BGP_HDR_MARKER_LENGTH);\n 3096 |   put_u16(buf+16, len);\n 3097 |   buf[18] = type;\n 3098 | \n 3099 |   int success = sk_send(sk, len);\n 3100 |   if (success && ((conn->state == BS_ESTABLISHED) || (conn->state == BS_OPENCONFIRM)))\n 3101 |     bgp_start_timer(conn->send_hold_timer, conn->send_hold_time);\n 3102 | \n 3103 |   return success;\n 3104 | }\n 3105 | \n 3106 | /**\n 3107 |  * bgp_fire_tx - transmit packets\n 3108 |  * @conn: connection\n 3109 |  *\n 3110 |  * Whenever the transmit buffers of the underlying TCP connection\n 3111 |  * are free and we have any packets queued for sending, the socket functions\n 3112 |  * call bgp_fire_tx() which takes care of selecting the highest priority packet\n 3113 |  * queued (Notification > Keepalive > Open > Update), assembling its header\n 3114 |  * and body and sending it to the connection.\n 3115 |  */\n 3116 | static int\n 3117 | bgp_fire_tx(struct bgp_conn *conn)\n 3118 | {\n 3119 |   struct bgp_proto *p = conn->bgp;\n 3120 |   struct bgp_channel *c;\n 3121 |   byte *buf, *pkt, *end;\n 3122 |   uint s;\n 3123 | \n 3124 |   if (!conn->sk)\n 3125 |     return 0;\n 3126 | \n 3127 |   buf = conn->sk->tbuf;\n 3128 |   pkt = buf + BGP_HEADER_LENGTH;\n 3129 |   s = conn->packets_to_send;\n 3130 | \n 3131 |   if (s & (1 << PKT_SCHEDULE_CLOSE))\n 3132 |   {\n 3133 |     /* We can finally close connection and enter idle state */\n 3134 |     bgp_conn_enter_idle_state(conn);\n 3135 |     return 0;\n 3136 |   }\n 3137 |   if (s & (1 << PKT_NOTIFICATION))\n 3138 |   {\n 3139 |     conn->packets_to_send = 1 << PKT_SCHEDULE_CLOSE;\n 3140 |     end = bgp_create_notification(conn, pkt);\n 3141 |     return bgp_send(conn, PKT_NOTIFICATION, end - buf);\n 3142 |   }\n 3143 |   else if (s & (1 << PKT_OPEN))\n 3144 |   {\n 3145 |     conn->packets_to_send &= ~(1 << PKT_OPEN);\n 3146 |     end = bgp_create_open(conn, pkt);\n 3147 | \n 3148 |     conn->local_open_msg = bgp_copy_open(p, buf, end - buf);\n 3149 |     conn->local_open_length = end - buf - BGP_HEADER_LENGTH;\n 3150 | \n 3151 |     return bgp_send(conn, PKT_OPEN, end - buf);\n 3152 |   }\n 3153 |   else if (s & (1 << PKT_KEEPALIVE))\n 3154 |   {\n 3155 |     conn->packets_to_send &= ~(1 << PKT_KEEPALIVE);\n 3156 |     BGP_TRACE(D_PACKETS, \"Sending KEEPALIVE\");\n 3157 |     bgp_start_timer(conn->keepalive_timer, conn->keepalive_time);\n 3158 |     return bgp_send(conn, PKT_KEEPALIVE, BGP_HEADER_LENGTH);\n 3159 |   }\n 3160 |   else while (conn->channels_to_send)\n 3161 |   {\n 3162 |     c = bgp_get_channel_to_send(p, conn);\n 3163 |     s = c->packets_to_send;\n 3164 | \n 3165 |     if (s & (1 << PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH))\n 3166 |     {\n 3167 |       c->packets_to_send &= ~(1 << PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH);\n 3168 |       end = bgp_create_route_refresh(c, pkt);\n 3169 |       return bgp_send(conn, PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH, end - buf);\n 3170 |     }\n 3171 |     else if (s & (1 << PKT_BEGIN_REFRESH))\n 3172 |     {\n 3173 |       /* BoRR is a subtype of RR, but uses separate bit in packets_to_send */\n 3174 |       c->packets_to_send &= ~(1 << PKT_BEGIN_REFRESH);\n 3175 |       end = bgp_create_begin_refresh(c, pkt);\n 3176 |       return bgp_send(conn, PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH, end - buf);\n 3177 |     }\n 3178 |     else if (s & (1 << PKT_UPDATE))\n 3179 |     {\n 3180 |       end = bgp_create_update(c, pkt);\n 3181 |       if (end)\n 3182 | \treturn bgp_send(conn, PKT_UPDATE, end - buf);\n 3183 | \n 3184 |       /* No update to send, perhaps we need to send End-of-RIB or EoRR */\n 3185 |       c->packets_to_send = 0;\n 3186 |       conn->channels_to_send &= ~(1 << c->index);\n 3187 | \n 3188 |       if (c->feed_state == BFS_LOADED)\n 3189 |       {\n 3190 | \tc->feed_state = BFS_NONE;\n 3191 | \tend = bgp_create_end_mark(c, pkt);\n 3192 | \treturn bgp_send(conn, PKT_UPDATE, end - buf);\n 3193 |       }\n 3194 | \n 3195 |       else if (c->feed_state == BFS_REFRESHED)\n 3196 |       {\n 3197 | \tc->feed_state = BFS_NONE;\n 3198 | \tend = bgp_create_end_refresh(c, pkt);\n 3199 | \treturn bgp_send(conn, PKT_ROUTE_REFRESH, end - buf);\n 3200 |       }\n 3201 |     }\n 3202 |     else if (s)\n 3203 |       bug(\"Channel packets_to_send: %x\", s);\n 3204 | \n 3205 |     c->packets_to_send = 0;\n 3206 |     conn->channels_to_send &= ~(1 << c->index);\n 3207 |   }\n 3208 | \n 3209 |   return 3477 | \n 3478 | /**\n 3479 |  * bgp_rx_packet - handle a received packet\n 3480 |  * @conn: BGP connection\n 3481 |  * @pkt: start of the packet\n 3482 |  * @len: packet size\n 3483 |  *\n 3484 |  * @size: amount of data received\n 3514 |  *\n 3515 |  * bgp_rx() is called by the socket layer whenever new data arrive from\n 3516 |  * the underlying TCP connection.
