9836:        "source_code": "    1 | /*\n    2 |  *\tBIRD Library -- Flow specification (RFC 8955)\n    3 |  *\n    4 |  *\t(c) 2016 CZ.NIC z.s.p.o.\n    5 |  *\n    6 |  *\tCan be freely distributed and used under the terms of the GNU GPL.\n    7 |  */\n    8 | \n    9 | /**\n   10 |  * DOC: Flow specification (flowspec)\n   11 |  *\n   12 |  * Flowspec are rules (RFC 8955) for firewalls disseminated using BGP protocol.\n   13 |  * The |flowspec.c| is a library for handling flowspec binary streams and\n   14 |  * flowspec data structures. You will find there functions for validation\n   15 |  * incoming flowspec binary streams, iterators for jumping over components,\n   16 |  * functions for handling a length and functions for formatting flowspec data\n   17 |  * structure into user-friendly text representation.\n   18 |  *\n   19 |  * In this library, you will find also flowspec builder. In |confbase.Y|, there\n   20 |  * are grammar's rules for parsing and building new flowspec data structure\n   21 |  * from BIRD's configuration files and from BIRD's command line interface.\n   22 |  * Finalize function will assemble final &net_addr_flow4 or &net_addr_flow6\n   23 |  * data structure.\n   24 |  *\n   25 |  * The data structures &net_addr_flow4 and &net_addr_flow6 are defined in\n   26 |  * |net.h| file. The attribute length is size of whole data structure plus\n   27 |  * binary stream representation of flowspec including a compressed encoded\n   28 |  * length of flowspec.\n   29 |  *\n   30 |  * Sometimes in code, it is used expression flowspec type, it should mean\n   31 |  * flowspec component type.\n   32 |  */\n   33 | \n   34 | #include <stdlib.h>\n   35 | \n   36 | #include \"nest/bird.h\"\n   37 | #include \"lib/flowspec.h\"\n   38 | #include \"conf/conf.h\"\n   39 | \n   40 | \n   41 | static const char* flow4_type_str[] = {\n   42 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX]\t= \"dst\",\n   43 |   [FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PREFIX]\t= \"src\",\n   44 |   [FLOW_TYPE_IP_PROTOCOL]\t= \"proto\",\n   45 |   [FLOW_TYPE_PORT]\t\t= \"port\",\n   46 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DST_PORT]\t\t= \"dport\",\n   47 |   [FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PORT]\t\t= \"sport\",\n   48 |   [FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_TYPE]\t\t= \"icmp type\",\n   49 |   [FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_CODE]\t\t= \"icmp code\",\n   50 |   [FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS]\t\t= \"tcp flags\",\n   51 |   [FLOW_TYPE_PACKET_LENGTH]\t= \"length\",\n   52 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DSCP]\t\t= \"dscp\",\n   53 |   [FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT]\t\t= \"fragment\"\n   54 | };\n   55 | \n   56 | static const char* flow6_type_str[] = {\n   57 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX]\t= \"dst\",\n   58 |   [FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PREFIX]\t= \"src\",\n   59 |   [FLOW_TYPE_NEXT_HEADER]\t= \"next header\",\n   60 |   [FLOW_TYPE_PORT]\t\t= \"port\",\n   61 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DST_PORT]\t\t= \"dport\",\n   62 |   [FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PORT]\t\t= \"sport\",\n   63 |   [FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_TYPE]\t\t= \"icmp type\",\n   64 |   [FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_CODE]\t\t= \"icmp code\",\n   65 |   [FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS]\t\t= \"tcp flags\",\n   66 |   [FLOW_TYPE_PACKET_LENGTH]\t= \"length\",\n   67 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DSCP]\t\t= \"dscp\",\n   68 |   [FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT]\t\t= \"fragment\",\n   69 |   [FLOW_TYPE_LABEL]\t\t= \"label\"\n   70 | };\n   71 | \n   72 | /**\n   73 |  * flow_type_str - get stringified flowspec name of component\n   74 |  * @type: flowspec component type\n   75 |  * @ipv6: IPv4/IPv6 decide flag, use zero for IPv4 and one for IPv6\n   76 |  *\n   77 |  * This function returns flowspec name of component @type in string.\n   78 |  */\n   79 | const char *\n   80 | flow_type_str(enum flow_type type, int ipv6)\n   81 | {\n   82 |   return ipv6 ? flow6_type_str[type] : flow4_type_str[type];\n   83 | }\n   84 | \n   85 | /*\n   86 |  * \tLength\n   87 |  */\n   88 | \n   89 | /**\n   90 |  * flow_write_length - write compressed length value\n   91 |  * @data: destination buffer to write\n   92 |  * @len: the value of the length (0 to 0xfff) for writing\n   93 |  *\n   94 |  * This function writes appropriate as (1- or 2-bytes) the value of @len into\n   95 |  * buffer @data. The function returns number of written bytes, thus 1 or 2 bytes.\n   96 |  */\n   97 | uint\n   98 | flow_write_length(byte *data, u16 len)\n   99 | {\n  100 |   if (len >= 0xf0)\n  101 |   {\n  102 |     put_u16(data, len | 0xf000);\n  103 |     return 2;\n  104 |   }\n  105 | \n  106 |   *data = len;\n  107 |   return 1;\n  108 | }\n  109 | \n  110 | inline static uint\n  111 | get_value_length(const byte *op)\n  112 | {\n  113 |   return (1 << ((*op & 0x30) >> 4));\n  114 | }\n  115 | \n  116 | \n  117 | /*\n  118 |  *\tFlowspec iterators\n  119 |  */\n  120 | \n  121 | static inline u8  num_op(const byte *op)    { return  (*op & 0x07); }\n  122 | static inline int isset_and(const byte *op) { return ((*op & 0x40) == 0x40); }\n  123 | static inline int isset_end(const byte *op) { return ((*op & 0x80) == 0x80); }\n  124 | \n  125 | static const byte *\n  126 | flow_first_part(const byte *data)\n  127 | {\n  128 |   if (!data || flow_read_length(data) == 0)\n  129 |     return NULL;\n  130 | \n  131 |   /* It is allowed to encode the value of length less then 240 into 2-bytes too */\n  132 |   if ((data[0] & 0xf0) == 0xf0)\n  133 |     return data + 2;\n  134 | \n  135 |   return data + 1;\n  136 | }\n  137 | \n  138 | /**\n  139 |  * flow4_first_part - get position of the first flowspec component\n  140 |  * @f: flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow4\n  141 |  *\n  142 |  * This function return a position to the beginning of the first flowspec\n  143 |  * component in IPv4 flowspec @f.\n  144 |  */\n  145 | inline const byte *\n  146 | flow4_first_part(const net_addr_flow4 *f)\n  147 | {\n  148 |   return f ? flow_first_part(f->data) : NULL;\n  149 | }\n  150 | \n  151 | /**\n  152 |  * flow6_first_part - get position of the first flowspec component\n  153 |  * @f: flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow6\n  154 |  *\n  155 |  * This function return a position to the beginning of the first flowspec\n  156 |  * component in IPv6 flowspec @f.\n  157 |  */\n  158 | inline const byte *\n  159 | flow6_first_part(const net_addr_flow6 *f)\n  160 | {\n  161 |   return f ? flow_first_part(f->data) : NULL;\n  162 | }\n  163 | \n  164 | static const byte *\n  165 | flow_next_part(const byte *pos, const byte *end, int ipv6)\n  166 | {\n  167 |   switch (*pos++)\n  168 |   {\n  169 |   case FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX:\n  170 |   case FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PREFIX:\n  171 |   {\n  172 |     uint pxlen = *pos++;\n  173 |     uint bytes = BYTES(pxlen);\n  174 |     if (ipv6)\n  175 |     {\n  176 |       uint offset = *pos++ / 8;\n  177 |       pos += bytes - offset;\n  178 |     }\n  179 |     else\n  180 |     {\n  181 |       pos += bytes;\n  182 |     }\n  183 |     break;\n  184 |   }\n  185 | \n  186 |   case FLOW_TYPE_IP_PROTOCOL: /* == FLOW_TYPE_NEXT_HEADER */\n  187 |   case FLOW_TYPE_PORT:\n  188 |   case FLOW_TYPE_DST_PORT:\n  189 |   case FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PORT:\n  190 |   case FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_TYPE:\n  191 |   case FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_CODE:\n  192 |   case FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS:\n  193 |   case FLOW_TYPE_PACKET_LENGTH:\n  194 |   case FLOW_TYPE_DSCP:\n  195 |   case FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT:\n  196 |   case FLOW_TYPE_LABEL:\n  197 |   {\n  198 |     /* Is this the end of list operator-value pair? */\n  199 |     uint last = 0;\n  200 | \n  201 |     while (!last)\n  202 |     {\n  203 |       last = isset_end(pos);\n  204 | \n  205 |       /* Value length of operator */\n  206 |       uint len = get_value_length(pos);\n  207 |       pos += 1+len;\n  208 |     }\n  209 |     break;\n  210 |   }\n  211 |   default:\n  212 |     return NULL;\n  213 |   }\n  214 | \n  215 |   return (pos < end) ? pos : NULL;\n  216 | }\n  217 | \n  218 | /**\n  219 |  * flow4_next_part - an iterator over flowspec components in flowspec binary stream\n  220 |  * @pos: the beginning of a previous or the first component in flowspec binary\n  221 |  *       stream\n  222 |  * @end: the last valid byte in scanned flowspec binary stream\n  223 |  *\n  224 |  * This function returns a position to the beginning of the next component\n  225 |  * (to a component type byte) in flowspec binary stream or %NULL for the end.\n  226 |  */\n  227 | inline const byte *\n  228 | flow4_next_part(const byte *pos, const byte *end)\n  229 | {\n  230 |   return flow_next_part(pos, end, 0);\n  231 | }\n  232 | \n  233 | /**\n  234 |  * flow6_next_part - an iterator over flowspec components in flowspec binary stream\n  235 |  * @pos: the beginning of a previous or the first component in flowspec binary\n  236 |  *       stream\n  237 |  * @end: the last valid byte in scanned flowspec binary stream\n  238 |  *\n  239 |  * This function returns a position to the beginning of the next component\n  240 |  * (to a component type byte) in flowspec binary stream or %NULL for the end.\n  241 |  */\n  242 | inline const byte *\n  243 | flow6_next_part(const byte *pos, const byte *end)\n  244 | {\n  245 |   return flow_next_part(pos, end, 1);\n  246 | }\n  247 | \n  248 | static const byte *\n  249 | flow_get_part(const byte *data, uint dlen, uint type, int ipv6)\n  250 | {\n  251 |   const byte *part;\n  252 | \n  253 |   for (part = flow_first_part(data);\n  254 |        part && (part[0] <= type);\n  255 |        part = flow_next_part(part, data+dlen, ipv6))\n  256 |     if (part[0] == type)\n  257 |       return part;\n  258 | \n  259 |   return NULL;\n  260 | }\n  261 | \n  262 | const byte *\n  263 | flow4_get_part(const net_addr_flow4 *f, uint type)\n  264 | {\n  265 |   return flow_get_part(f->data, f->length - sizeof(net_addr_flow4), type, 0);\n  266 | }\n  267 | \n  268 | const byte *\n  269 | flow6_get_part(const net_addr_flow6 *f, uint type)\n  270 | {\n  271 |   return flow_get_part(f->data, f->length - sizeof(net_addr_flow6), type, 1);\n  272 | }\n  273 | \n  274 | \n  275 | /*\n  276 |  *\tFlowspec accessors\n  277 |  */\n  278 | \n  279 | static inline ip4_addr\n  280 | flow_read_ip4(const byte *px, uint pxlen)\n  281 | {\n  282 |   ip4_addr ip = IP4_NONE;\n  283 |   memcpy(&ip, px, BYTES(pxlen));\n  284 |   return ip4_ntoh(ip);\n  285 | }\n  286 | \n  287 | ip4_addr\n  288 | flow_read_ip4_part(const byte *part)\n  289 | {\n  290 |   return flow_read_ip4(part + 2, part[1]);\n  291 | }\n  292 | \n  293 | static inline ip6_addr\n  294 | flow_read_ip6(const byte *px, uint pxlen, uint pxoffset)\n  295 | {\n  296 |   ip6_addr ip = IP6_NONE;\n  297 |   memcpy(&ip, px, BYTES(pxlen - pxoffset));\n  298 |   return ip6_shift_right(ip6_ntoh(ip), pxoffset);\n  299 | }\n  300 | \n  301 | ip6_addr\n  302 | flow_read_ip6_part(const byte *part)\n  303 | {\n  304 |   return flow_read_ip6(part + 3, part[1], part[2]);\n  305 | }\n  306 | \n  307 | static uint\n  308 | get_value(const byte *val, u8 len)\n  309 | {\n  310 |   switch (len)\n  311 |   {\n  312 |   case 1: return *val;\n  313 |   case 2: return get_u16(val);\n  314 |   case 4: return get_u32(val);\n  315 |   // No component may have length 8\n  316 |   // case 8: return get_u64(val);\n  317 |   }\n  318 | \n  319 |   return 0;\n  320 | }\n  321 | \n  322 | \n  323 | /*\n  324 |  * \tFlowspec validation\n  325 |  */\n  326 | \n  327 | static const char* flow_validated_state_str_[] = {\n  328 |   [FLOW_ST_UNKNOWN_COMPONENT] \t\t= \"Unknown component\",\n  329 |   [FLOW_ST_VALID] \t\t\t= \"Valid\",\n  330 |   [FLOW_ST_NOT_COMPLETE] \t\t= \"Not complete\",\n  331 |   [FLOW_ST_EXCEED_MAX_PREFIX_LENGTH] \t= \"Exceed maximal prefix length\",\n  332 |   [FLOW_ST_EXCEED_MAX_PREFIX_OFFSET]\t= \"Exceed maximal prefix offset\",\n  333 |   [FLOW_ST_EXCEED_MAX_VALUE_LENGTH]\t= \"Exceed maximal value length\",\n  334 |   [FLOW_ST_BAD_TYPE_ORDER] \t\t= \"Bad component order\",\n  335 |   [FLOW_ST_AND_BIT_SHOULD_BE_UNSET] \t= \"The AND-bit should be unset\",\n  336 |   [FLOW_ST_ZERO_BIT_SHOULD_BE_UNSED] \t= \"The Zero-bit should be unset\",\n  337 |   [FLOW_ST_DEST_PREFIX_REQUIRED] \t= \"Destination prefix is missing\",\n  338 |   [FLOW_ST_INVALID_TCP_FLAGS]\t\t= \"TCP flags exceeding 0xfff\",\n  339 |   [FLOW_ST_CANNOT_USE_DONT_FRAGMENT]    = \"Cannot use Don't fragment flag in IPv6 flow\"\n  340 | };\n  341 | \n  342 | /**\n  343 |  * flow_validated_state_str - return a textual description of validation process\n  344 |  * @code: validation result\n  345 |  *\n  346 |  * This function return well described validation state in string.\n  347 |  */\n  348 | const char *\n  349 | flow_validated_state_str(enum flow_validated_state code)\n  350 | {\n  351 |   return flow_validated_state_str_[code];\n  352 | }\n  353 | \n  354 | static const u8 flow4_max_value_length[] = {\n  355 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX]\t= 0,\n  356 |   [FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PREFIX]\t= 0,\n  357 |   [FLOW_TYPE_IP_PROTOCOL]\t= 1,\n  358 |   [FLOW_TYPE_PORT]\t\t= 2,\n  359 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DST_PORT]\t\t= 2,\n  360 |   [FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PORT]\t\t= 2,\n  361 |   [FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_TYPE]\t\t= 1,\n  362 |   [FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_CODE]\t\t= 1,\n  363 |   [FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS]\t\t= 2,\n  364 |   [FLOW_TYPE_PACKET_LENGTH]\t= 2,\n  365 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DSCP]\t\t= 1,\n  366 |   [FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT]\t\t= 1\t/* XXX */\n  367 | };\n  368 | \n  369 | static const u8 flow6_max_value_length[] = {\n  370 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX]\t= 0,\n  371 |   [FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PREFIX]\t= 0,\n  372 |   [FLOW_TYPE_NEXT_HEADER]\t= 1,\n  373 |   [FLOW_TYPE_PORT]\t\t= 2,\n  374 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DST_PORT]\t\t= 2,\n  375 |   [FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PORT]\t\t= 2,\n  376 |   [FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_TYPE]\t\t= 1,\n  377 |   [FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_CODE]\t\t= 1,\n  378 |   [FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS]\t\t= 2,\n  379 |   [FLOW_TYPE_PACKET_LENGTH]\t= 2,\n  380 |   [FLOW_TYPE_DSCP]\t\t= 1,\n  381 |   [FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT]\t\t= 1,\t/* XXX */\n  382 |   [FLOW_TYPE_LABEL]\t\t= 4\n  383 | };\n  384 | \n  385 | static u8\n  386 | flow_max_value_length(enum flow_type type, int ipv6)\n  387 | {\n  388 |   return ipv6 ? flow6_max_value_length[type] : flow4_max_value_length[type];\n  389 | }\n  390 | \n  391 | /**\n  392 |  * flow_check_cf_bmk_values - check value/bitmask part of flowspec component\n  393 |  * @fb: flow builder instance\n  394 |  * @neg: negation operand\n  395 |  * @val: value from value/mask pair\n  396 |  * @mask: bitmap mask from value/mask pair\n  397 |  *\n  398 |  * This function checks value/bitmask pair. If some problem will appear, the\n  399 |  * function calls cf_error() function with a textual description of reason\n  400 |  * to failing of validation.\n  401 |  */\n  402 | void\n  403 | flow_check_cf_bmk_values(struct flow_builder *fb, u8 neg, u32 val, u32 mask)\n  404 | {\n  405 |   flow_check_cf_value_length(fb, val);\n  406 |   flow_check_cf_value_length(fb, mask);\n  407 | \n  408 |   if (neg && !(val == 0 || val == mask))\n  409 |     cf_error(\"For negation, value must be zero or bitmask\");\n  410 | \n  411 |   if ((fb->this_type == FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS) && (mask & 0xf000))\n  412 |     cf_error(\"Invalid mask 0x%x, must not exceed 0xfff\", mask);\n  413 | \n  414 |   if ((fb->this_type == FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT) && fb->ipv6 && (mask & 0x01))\n  415 |     cf_error(\"Invalid mask 0x%x, bit 0 must be 0\", mask);\n  416 | \n  417 |   if (val & ~mask)\n  418 |     cf_error(\"Value 0x%x outside bitmask 0x%x\", val, mask);\n  419 | }\n  420 | \n  421 | /**\n  422 |  * flow_check_cf_value_length - check value by flowspec component type\n  423 |  * @fb: flow builder instance\n  424 |  * @val: value\n  425 |  *\n  426 |  * This function checks if the value is in range of component's type support.\n  427 |  * If some problem will appear, the function calls cf_error() function with\n  428 |  * a textual description of reason to failing of validation.\n  429 |  */\n  430 | void\n  431 | flow_check_cf_value_length(struct flow_builder *fb, u32 val)\n  432 | {\n  433 |   enum flow_type t = fb->this_type;\n  434 |   u8 max = flow_max_value_length(t, fb->ipv6);\n  435 | \n  436 |   if (t == FLOW_TYPE_DSCP && val > 0x3f)\n  437 |     cf_error(\"%s value %u out of range (0-63)\", flow_type_str(t, fb->ipv6), val);\n  438 | \n  439 |   if (max == 1 && (val > 0xff))\n  440 |     cf_error(\"%s value %u out of range (0-255)\", flow_type_str(t, fb->ipv6), val);\n  441 | \n  442 |   if (max == 2 && (val > 0xffff))\n  443 |     cf_error(\"%s value %u out of range (0-65535)\", flow_type_str(t, fb->ipv6), val);\n  444 | }\n  445 | \n  446 | static enum flow_validated_state\n  447 | flow_validate(const byte *nlri, uint len, int ipv6)\n  448 | {\n  449 |   enum flow_type type = 0;\n  450 |   const byte *pos = nlri;\n  451 |   const byte *end = nlri + len;\n  452 | \n  453 |   while (pos < end)\n  454 |   {\n  455 |     /* Check increasing type ordering */\n  456 |     if (*pos <= type)\n  457 |       return FLOW_ST_BAD_TYPE_ORDER;\n  458 |     type = *pos++;\n  459 | \n  460 |     switch (type)\n  461 |     {\n  462 |     case FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX:\n  463 |     case FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PREFIX:\n  464 |     {\n  465 |       uint pxlen = *pos++;\n  466 |       if (pxlen > (ipv6 ? IP6_MAX_PREFIX_LENGTH : IP4_MAX_PREFIX_LENGTH))\n  467 | \treturn FLOW_ST_EXCEED_MAX_PREFIX_LENGTH;\n  468 | \n  469 |       uint bytes = BYTES(pxlen);\n  470 |       if (ipv6)\n  471 |       {\n  472 |         uint pxoffset = *pos++;\n  473 |         if (pxoffset > IP6_MAX_PREFIX_LENGTH || pxoffset > pxlen)\n  474 |           return FLOW_ST_EXCEED_MAX_PREFIX_OFFSET;\n  475 |         bytes = BYTES(pxlen - pxoffset);\n  476 |       }\n  477 |       pos += bytes;\n  478 | \n  479 |       break;\n  480 |     }\n  481 | \n  482 |     case FLOW_TYPE_LABEL:\n  483 |       if (!ipv6)\n  484 | \treturn FLOW_ST_UNKNOWN_COMPONENT;\n  485 |       /* fall through */\n  486 |     case FLOW_TYPE_IP_PROTOCOL: /* == FLOW_TYPE_NEXT_HEADER */\n  487 |     case FLOW_TYPE_PORT:\n  488 |     case FLOW_TYPE_DST_PORT:\n  489 |     case FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PORT:\n  490 |     case FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_TYPE:\n  491 |     case FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_CODE:\n  492 |     case FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS:\n  493 |     case FLOW_TYPE_PACKET_LENGTH:\n  494 |     case FLOW_TYPE_DSCP:\n  495 |     case FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT:\n  496 |     {\n  497 |       uint last = 0;\n  498 |       uint first = 1;\n  499 | \n  500 |       while (!last)\n  501 |       {\n  502 | \t/*\n  503 | \t *    0   1   2   3   4   5   6   7\n  504 | \t *  +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+\n  505 | \t *  | e | a |  len  | 0 |lt |gt |eq |\n  506 | \t *  +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+\n  507 | \t *\n  508 | \t *           Numeric operator\n  509 | \t */\n  510 | \n  511 | \tlast = isset_end(pos);\n  512 | \n  513 | \t/* The AND bit should in the first operator byte of a sequence */\n  514 | \tif (first && isset_and(pos))\n  515 | \t  return FLOW_ST_AND_BIT_SHOULD_BE_UNSET;\n  516 | \n  517 | \t/* This bit should be zero */\n  518 | \tif (*pos & 0x08)\n  519 | \t  return FLOW_ST_ZERO_BIT_SHOULD_BE_UNSED;\n  520 | \n  521 | \tif (type == FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS || type == FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT)\n  522 | \t{\n  523 | \t  /*\n  524 | \t   *    0   1   2   3   4   5   6   7\n  525 | \t   *  +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+\n  526 | \t   *  | e | a |  len  | 0 | 0 |not| m |\n  527 | \t   *  +---+---+---+---+---+---+---+---+\n  528 | \t   *\n  529 | \t   *           Bitmask operand\n  530 | \t   */\n  531 | \t  if (*pos & 0x04)\n  532 | \t    return FLOW_ST_ZERO_BIT_SHOULD_BE_UNSED;\n  533 | \t}\n  534 | \n  535 | \t/* Value length of operator */\n  536 | \tuint len = get_value_length(pos);\n  537 | \tif (len > flow_max_value_length(type, ipv6))\n  538 | \t  return FLOW_ST_EXCEED_MAX_VALUE_LENGTH;\n  539 | \n  540 | \t/* TCP Flags component must not check highest nibble (just 12 valid bits) */\n  541 | \tif ((type == FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS) && (len == 2) && (pos[1] & 0xf0))\n  542 | \t  return FLOW_ST_INVALID_TCP_FLAGS;\n  543 | \n  544 | \t/* Bit-7 must be 0 [draft-ietf-idr-flow-spec-v6] */\n  545 | \tif ((type == FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT) && ipv6 && (pos[1] & 0x01))\n  546 | \t  return FLOW_ST_CANNOT_USE_DONT_FRAGMENT;\n  547 | \t/* XXX: Could be a fragment component encoded in 2-bytes? */\n  548 | \n  549 | \tpos += 1+len;\n  550 | \n  551 | \tif (pos > end && !last)\n  552 | \t  return FLOW_ST_NOT_COMPLETE;\n  553 | \n  554 | \tif (pos > (end+1))\n  555 | \t  return FLOW_ST_NOT_COMPLETE;\n  556 | \n  557 | \tfirst = 0;\n  558 |       }\n  559 |       break;\n  560 |     }\n  561 |     default:\n  562 |       return FLOW_ST_UNKNOWN_COMPONENT;\n  563 |     }\n  564 |   }\n  565 | \n  566 |   if (pos != end)\n  567 |     return FLOW_ST_NOT_COMPLETE;\n  568 | \n  569 |   return FLOW_ST_VALID;\n  570 | }\n  571 | \n  572 | /**\n  573 |  * flow4_validate - check untrustworthy IPv4 flowspec data stream\n  574 |  * @nlri: flowspec data stream without compressed encoded length value\n  575 |  * @len: length of @nlri\n  576 |  *\n  577 |  * This function checks meaningfulness of binary flowspec. It should return\n  578 |  * %FLOW_ST_VALID or %FLOW_ST_UNKNOWN_COMPONENT. If some problem appears, it\n  579 |  * returns some other %FLOW_ST_xxx state.\n  580 |  */\n  581 | inline enum flow_validated_state\n  582 | flow4_validate(const byte *nlri, uint len)\n  583 | {\n  584 |   return flow_validate(nlri, len, 0);\n  585 | }\n  586 | \n  587 | /**\n  588 |  * flow6_validate - check untrustworthy IPv6 flowspec data stream\n  589 |  * @nlri: flowspec binary stream without encoded length value\n  590 |  * @len: length of @nlri\n  591 |  *\n  592 |  * This function checks meaningfulness of binary flowspec. It should return\n  593 |  * %FLOW_ST_VALID or %FLOW_ST_UNKNOWN_COMPONENT. If some problem appears, it\n  594 |  * returns some other %FLOW_ST_xxx state.\n  595 |  */\n  596 | inline enum flow_validated_state\n  597 | flow6_validate(const byte *nlri, uint len)\n  598 | {\n  599 |   return flow_validate(nlri, len, 1);\n  600 | }\n  601 | \n  602 | /**\n  603 |  * flow4_validate_cf - validate flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow4 in parsing time\n  604 |  * @f: flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow4\n  605 |  *\n  606 |  * Check if @f is valid flowspec data structure. Can call cf_error() function\n  607 |  * with a textual description of reason to failing of validation.\n  608 |  */\n  609 | void\n  610 | flow4_validate_cf(net_addr_flow4 *f)\n  611 | {\n  612 |   enum flow_validated_state r = flow4_validate(flow4_first_part(f), flow_read_length(f->data));\n  613 | \n  614 |   if (r != FLOW_ST_VALID)\n  615 |     cf_error(\"Invalid flow route: %s\", flow_validated_state_str(r));\n  616 | }\n  617 | \n  618 | /**\n  619 |  * flow6_validate_cf - validate flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow6 in parsing time\n  620 |  * @f: flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow6\n  621 |  *\n  622 |  * Check if @f is valid flowspec data structure. Can call cf_error() function\n  623 |  * with a textual description of reason to failing of validation.\n  624 |  */\n  625 | void\n  626 | flow6_validate_cf(net_addr_flow6 *f)\n  627 | {\n  628 |   enum flow_validated_state r = flow6_validate(flow6_first_part(f), flow_read_length(f->data));\n  629 | \n  630 |   if (r != FLOW_ST_VALID)\n  631 |     cf_error(\"Invalid flow route: %s\", flow_validated_state_str(r));\n  632 | }\n  633 | \n  634 | \n  635 | /*\n  636 |  * \tFlowspec Builder\n  637 |  */\n  638 | \n  639 | /**\n  640 |  * flow_builder_init - constructor for flowspec builder instance\n  641 |  * @pool: memory pool\n  642 |  *\n  643 |  * This function prepares flowspec builder instance using memory pool @pool.\n  644 |  */\n  645 | struct flow_builder *\n  646 | flow_builder_init(pool *pool)\n  647 | {\n  648 |   struct flow_builder *fb = mb_allocz(pool, sizeof(struct flow_builder));\n  649 |   BUFFER_INIT(fb->data, pool, 4);\n  650 |   return fb;\n  651 | }\n  652 | \n  653 | static int\n  654 | is_stackable_type(enum flow_type type)\n  655 | {\n  656 |   switch (type)\n  657 |   {\n  658 |   case FLOW_TYPE_IP_PROTOCOL:\n  659 |   case FLOW_TYPE_PORT:\n  660 |   case FLOW_TYPE_DST_PORT:\n  661 |   case FLOW_TYPE_SRC_PORT:\n  662 |   case FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_TYPE:\n  663 |   case FLOW_TYPE_ICMP_CODE:\n  664 |   case FLOW_TYPE_TCP_FLAGS:\n  665 |   case FLOW_TYPE_PACKET_LENGTH:\n  666 |   case FLOW_TYPE_DSCP:\n  667 |   case FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT:\n  668 |   case FLOW_TYPE_LABEL:\n  669 |     return 1;\n  670 | \n  671 |   default:\n  672 |     /* The unknown components are not stack-able in default */\n  673 |     return 0;\n  674 |   }\n  675 | }\n  676 | \n  677 | static int\n  678 | builder_add_prepare(struct flow_builder *fb)\n  679 | {\n  680 |   if (fb->parts[fb->this_type].length)\n  681 |   {\n  682 |     if (fb->last_type != fb->this_type)\n  683 |       return 0;\n  684 | \n  685 |     if (!is_stackable_type(fb->this_type))\n  686 |       return 0;\n  687 |   }\n  688 |   else\n  689 |   {\n  690 |     fb->parts[fb->this_type].offset = fb->data.used;\n  691 |   }\n  692 | \n  693 |   return 1;\n  694 | }\n  695 | \n  696 | static void\n  697 | builder_add_finish(struct flow_builder *fb)\n  698 | {\n  699 |   fb->parts[fb->this_type].length = fb->data.used - fb->parts[fb->this_type].offset;\n  700 |   flow_builder_set_type(fb, fb->this_type);\n  701 | }\n  702 | \n  703 | static void\n  704 | push_pfx_to_buffer(struct flow_builder *fb, u8 pxlen_bytes, byte *ip)\n  705 | {\n  706 |   for (int i = 0; i < pxlen_bytes; i++)\n  707 |     BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = *ip++;\n  708 | }\n  709 | \n  710 | /**\n  711 |  * flow_builder4_add_pfx - add IPv4 prefix\n  712 |  * @fb: flowspec builder instance\n  713 |  * @n4: net address of type IPv4\n  714 |  *\n  715 |  * This function add IPv4 prefix into flowspec builder instance.\n  716 |  */\n  717 | int\n  718 | flow_builder4_add_pfx(struct flow_builder *fb, const net_addr_ip4 *n4)\n  719 | {\n  720 |   if (!builder_add_prepare(fb))\n  721 |     return 0;\n  722 | \n  723 |   ip4_addr ip4 = ip4_hton(n4->prefix);\n  724 | \n  725 |   BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = fb->this_type;\n  726 |   BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = n4->pxlen;\n  727 |   push_pfx_to_buffer(fb, BYTES(n4->pxlen), (byte *) &ip4);\n  728 | \n  729 |   builder_add_finish(fb);\n  730 |   return 1;\n  731 | }\n  732 | \n  733 | /**\n  734 |  * flow_builder6_add_pfx - add IPv6 prefix\n  735 |  * @fb: flowspec builder instance\n  736 |  * @n6: net address of type IPv4\n  737 |  * @pxoffset: prefix offset for @n6\n  738 |  *\n  739 |  * This function add IPv4 prefix into flowspec builder instance. This function\n  740 |  * should return 1 for successful adding, otherwise returns %0.\n  741 |  */\n  742 | int\n  743 | flow_builder6_add_pfx(struct flow_builder *fb, const net_addr_ip6 *n6, u32 pxoffset)\n  744 | {\n  745 |   if (!builder_add_prepare(fb))\n  746 |     return 0;\n  747 | \n  748 |   ip6_addr ip6 = ip6_hton(ip6_shift_left(n6->prefix, pxoffset));\n  749 | \n  750 |   BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = fb->this_type;\n  751 |   BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = n6->pxlen;\n  752 |   BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = pxoffset;\n  753 |   push_pfx_to_buffer(fb, BYTES(n6->pxlen - pxoffset), ((byte *) &ip6));\n  754 | \n  755 |   builder_add_finish(fb);\n  756 |   return 1;\n  757 | }\n  758 | \n  759 | /**\n  760 |  * flow_builder_add_op_val - add operator/value pair\n  761 |  * @fb: flowspec builder instance\n  762 |  * @op: operator\n  763 |  * @value: value\n  764 |  *\n  765 |  * This function add operator/value pair as a part of a flowspec component. It\n  766 |  * is required to set appropriate flowspec component type using function\n  767 |  * flow_builder_set_type(). This function should return 1 for successful\n  768 |  * adding, otherwise returns 0.\n  769 |  */\n  770 | int\n  771 | flow_builder_add_op_val(struct flow_builder *fb, byte op, u32 value)\n  772 | {\n  773 |   if (!builder_add_prepare(fb))\n  774 |     return 0;\n  775 | \n  776 |   if (fb->this_type == fb->last_type)\n  777 |   {\n  778 |     /* Remove the end-bit from last operand-value pair of the component */\n  779 |     fb->data.data[fb->last_op_offset] &= 0x7f;\n  780 |   }\n  781 |   else\n  782 |   {\n  783 |     BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = fb->this_type;\n  784 |   }\n  785 | \n  786 |   fb->last_op_offset = fb->data.used;\n  787 | \n  788 |   /* Set the end-bit for operand-value pair of the component */\n  789 |   op |= 0x80;\n  790 | \n  791 |   if (value & 0xff00)\n  792 |   {\n  793 |     BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = op | 0x10;\n  794 |     put_u16(BUFFER_INC(fb->data, 2), value);\n  795 |   }\n  796 |   else\n  797 |   {\n  798 |     BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = op;\n  799 |     BUFFER_PUSH(fb->data) = (u8) value;\n  800 |   }\n  801 | \n  802 |   builder_add_finish(fb);\n  803 |   return 1;\n  804 | }\n  805 | \n  806 | /**\n  807 |  * flow_builder_add_val_mask - add value/bitmask pair\n  808 |  * @fb: flowspec builder instance\n  809 |  * @op: operator\n  810 |  * @value: value\n  811 |  * @mask: bitmask\n  812 |  *\n  813 |  * It is required to set appropriate flowspec component type using function\n  814 |  * flow_builder_set_type(). Note that for negation, value must be zero or equal\n  815 |  * to bitmask.\n  816 |  */\n  817 | int\n  818 | flow_builder_add_val_mask(struct flow_builder *fb, byte op, u32 value, u32 mask)\n  819 | {\n  820 |   u32 a =  value & mask;\n  821 |   u32 b = ~value & mask;\n  822 | \n  823 |   if (a)\n  824 |   {\n  825 |     flow_builder_add_op_val(fb, op ^ 0x01, a);\n  826 |     op |= FLOW_OP_AND;\n  827 |   }\n  828 | \n  829 |   if (b)\n  830 |     flow_builder_add_op_val(fb, op ^ 0x02, b);\n  831 | \n  832 |   return 1;\n  833 | }\n  834 | \n  835 | \n  836 | /**\n  837 |  * flow_builder_set_type - set type of next flowspec component\n  838 |  * @fb: flowspec builder instance\n  839 |  * @type: flowspec component type\n  840 |  *\n  841 |  * This function sets type of next flowspec component. It is necessary to call\n  842 |  * this function before each changing of adding flowspec component.\n  843 |  */\n  844 | void\n  845 | flow_builder_set_type(struct flow_builder *fb, enum flow_type type)\n  846 | {\n  847 |   fb->last_type = fb->this_type;\n  848 |   fb->this_type = type;\n  849 | }\n  850 | \n  851 | static void\n  852 | builder_write_parts(struct flow_builder *fb, byte *buf)\n  853 | {\n  854 |   for (int i = 1; i < FLOW_TYPE_MAX; i++)\n  855 |   {\n  856 |     if (fb->parts[i].length)\n  857 |     {\n  858 |       memcpy(buf, fb->data.data + fb->parts[i].offset, fb->parts[i].length);\n  859 |       buf += fb->parts[i].length;\n  860 |     }\n  861 |   }\n  862 | }\n  863 | \n  864 | /**\n  865 |  * flow_builder4_finalize - assemble final flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow4\n  866 |  * @fb: flowspec builder instance\n  867 |  * @lpool: linear memory pool\n  868 |  *\n  869 |  * This function returns final flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow4 allocated\n  870 |  * onto @lpool linear memory pool.\n  871 |  */\n  872 | net_addr_flow4 *\n  873 | flow_builder4_finalize(struct flow_builder *fb, linpool *lpool)\n  874 | {\n  875 |   uint data_len = fb->data.used + (fb->data.used < 0xf0 ? 1 : 2);\n  876 |   net_addr_flow4 *f = lp_alloc(lpool, sizeof(struct net_addr_flow4) + data_len);\n  877 | \n  878 |   ip4_addr prefix = IP4_NONE;\n  879 |   uint pxlen = 0;\n  880 | \n  881 |   if (fb->parts[FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX].length)\n  882 |   {\n  883 |     byte *part = fb->data.data + fb->parts[FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX].offset;\n  884 |     prefix = flow_read_ip4_part(part);\n  885 |     pxlen = flow_read_pxlen(part);\n  886 |   }\n  887 |   *f = NET_ADDR_FLOW4(prefix, pxlen, data_len);\n  888 | \n  889 |   builder_write_parts(fb, f->data + flow_write_length(f->data, fb->data.used));\n  890 | \n  891 |   return f;\n  892 | }\n  893 | \n  894 | /**\n  895 |  * flow_builder6_finalize - assemble final flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow6\n  896 |  * @fb: flowspec builder instance\n  897 |  * @lpool: linear memory pool for allocation of\n  898 |  *\n  899 |  * This function returns final flowspec data structure &net_addr_flow6 allocated\n  900 |  * onto @lpool linear memory pool.\n  901 |  */\n  902 | net_addr_flow6 *\n  903 | flow_builder6_finalize(struct flow_builder *fb, linpool *lpool)\n  904 | {\n  905 |   uint data_len =  fb->data.used + (fb->data.used < 0xf0 ? 1 : 2);\n  906 |   net_addr_flow6 *n = lp_alloc(lpool, sizeof(net_addr_flow6) + data_len);\n  907 | \n  908 |   ip6_addr prefix = IP6_NONE;\n  909 |   uint pxlen = 0;\n  910 | \n  911 |   if (fb->parts[FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX].length)\n  912 |   {\n  913 |     byte *part = fb->data.data + fb->parts[FLOW_TYPE_DST_PREFIX].offset;\n  914 |     prefix = flow_read_ip6_part(part);\n  915 |     pxlen = flow_read_pxlen(part);\n  916 |   }\n  917 |   *n = NET_ADDR_FLOW6(prefix, pxlen, data_len);\n  918 | \n  919 |   builder_write_parts(fb, n->data + flow_write_length(n->data, fb->data.used));\n  920 | \n  921 |   return n;\n  922 | }\n  923 | \n  924 | /**\n  925 |  * flow_builder_clear - flush flowspec builder instance for another flowspec creation\n  926 |  * @fb: flowspec builder instance\n  927 |  *\n  928 |  * This function flushes all data from builder but it maintains pre-allocated\n  929 |  * buffer space.\n  930 |  */\n  931 | void\n  932 | flow_builder_clear(struct flow_builder *fb)\n  933 | {\n  934 |   BUFFER(byte) data;\n  935 |   BUFFER_FLUSH(fb->data);\n  936 | \n  937 |   BUFFER_SHALLOW_COPY(data, fb->data);\n  938 |   memset(fb, 0, sizeof(struct flow_builder));\n  939 |   BUFFER_SHALLOW_COPY(fb->data, data);\n  940 | }\n  941 | \n  942 | \n  943 | /*\n  944 |  *\tFlowspec explication\n  945 |  */\n  946 | \n  947 | /**\n  948 |  * flow_explicate_buffer_size - return buffer size needed for explication\n  949 |  * @part: flowspec part to explicate\n  950 |  *\n  951 |  * This function computes and returns a required buffer size that has to be\n  952 |  * preallocated and passed to flow_explicate_part(). Note that it returns number\n  953 |  * of records, not number of bytes.\n  954 |  */\n  955 | uint\n  956 | flow_explicate_buffer_size(const byte *part)\n  957 | {\n  958 |   const byte *pos = part + 1;\n  959 |   uint first = 1;\n  960 |   uint len = 0;\n  961 | \n  962 |   while (1)\n  963 |   {\n  964 |     /*\n  965 |      * Conjunction sequences represent (mostly) one interval, do not count\n  966 |      * additional AND-ed operators. Ignore AND bit for the first operator.\n  967 |      */\n  968 |     if (!isset_and(pos) || first)\n  969 |       len++;\n  970 | \n  971 |     /*\n  972 |      * The exception is that NEQ operator adds one more interval (by splitting\n  973 |      * one of intervals defined by other operators).\n  974 |      */\n  975 |     if (num_op(pos) == FLOW_OP_NEQ)\n  976 |       len++;\n  977 | \n  978 |     if (isset_end(pos))\n  979 |       break;\n  980 | \n  981 |     first = 0;\n  982 |     pos = pos + 1 + get_value_length(pos);\n  983 |   }\n  984 | \n  985 |   return len;\n  986 | }\n  987 | \n  988 | static int flow_uint_cmp(const void *p1, const void *p2)\n  989 | { return uint_cmp(* (const uint *) p1, * (const uint *) p2); }\n  990 | \n  991 | /**\n  992 |  * flow_explicate_part - compute explicit interval list from flowspec part\n  993 |  * @part: flowspec part to explicate\n  994 |  * @buf: pre-allocated buffer for result\n  995 |  *\n  996 |  * This function analyzes a flowspec part with numeric operators (e.g. port) and\n  997 |  * computes an explicit interval list of allowed values. The result is written\n  998 |  * to provided buffer @buf, which must have space for enough interval records as\n  999 |  * returned by flow_explicate_buffer_size(). The intervals are represented as\n 1000 |  * two-sized arrays of lower and upper bound, both including. The return value\n 1001 |  * is the number of intervals in the buffer.\n 1002 |  */\n 1003 | uint\n 1004 | flow_explicate_part(const byte *part, uint (*buf)[2])\n 1005 | {\n 1006 |   /*\n 1007 |    * The Flowspec numeric expression is almost in DNF form (as union of\n 1008 |    * intersections), where each operator represents one elementary interval.\n 1009 |    * The exception is NEQ operator, which represents union of two intervals,\n 1010 |    * separated by the excluded value. Naive algorithm would be like:\n 1011 |    *\n 1012 |    * A <- empty set of intervals\n 1013 |    * for each sequence of operators in conjunction\n 1014 |    * {\n 1015 |    *   B <- empty set of intervals\n 1016 |    *   for each operator in the current sequence\n 1017 |    *   {\n 1018 |    *     C <- one or two elementary intervals from the current operator\n 1019 |    *     B <- intersection(B, C)\n 1020 |    *   }\n 1021 |    *   A <- union(A, B)\n 1022 |    * }\n 1023 |    *\n 1024 |    * We simplify this by representing B just as one interval (vars lo, hi) and a\n 1025 |    * list of excluded values. After the inner cycle, we expand that to a proper\n 1026 |    * list of intervals that is added to existing ones from previous cycles.\n 1027 |    * Finally, we sort and merge intersecting or touching intervals in A.\n 1028 |    *\n 1029 |    * The code handles up to 32bit values in numeric operators. Intervals are\n 1030 |    * represented by lower and upper bound, both including. Intermediate values\n 1031 |    * use s64 to simplify representation of excluding bounds for 0 and UINT32_MAX.\n 1032 |    */\n 1033 | \n 1034 |   const byte *pos = part + 1;\n 1035 |   const s64 max = 0xffffffff;\n 1036 |   s64 lo = 0;\n 1037 |   s64 hi = max;\n 1038 |   uint num = 0;\n 1039 |   uint neqs = 0;\n 1040 | \n 1041 |   /* Step 1 - convert conjunction sequences to lists of intervals */\n 1042 |   while (1)\n 1043 |   {\n 1044 |     uint op = num_op(pos);\n 1045 |     uint len = get_value_length(pos);\n 1046 |     s64  val = get_value(pos + 1, len);\n 1047 |     uint last = isset_end(pos);\n 1048 |     const byte *next_pos = pos + 1 + len;\n 1049 | \n 1050 |     /* Get a new interval from this operator */\n 1051 |     s64 nlo = (op & FLOW_OP_LT) ? 0   : ((op & FLOW_OP_EQ) ? val : (val + 1));\n 1052 |     s64 nhi = (op & FLOW_OP_GT) ? max : ((op & FLOW_OP_EQ) ? val : (val - 1));\n 1053 | \n 1054 |     /* Restrict current interval */\n 1055 |     lo = MAX(lo, nlo);\n 1056 |     hi = MIN(hi, nhi);\n 1057 | \n 1058 |     /* Store NEQs for later */\n 1059 |     if (op == FLOW_OP_NEQ)\n 1060 |     {\n 1061 |       buf[num + neqs][0] = val;\n 1062 |       buf[num + neqs][1] = 0;\n 1063 |       neqs++;\n 1064 |     }\n 1065 | \n 1066 |     /* End of conjunction sequence */\n 1067 |     if (last || !isset_and(next_pos))\n 1068 |     {\n 1069 |       if (neqs)\n 1070 |       {\n 1071 | \t/* Sort stored NEQs */\n 1072 | \tqsort(buf + num, neqs, 2 * sizeof(uint), flow_uint_cmp);\n 1073 | \n 1074 | \t/* Dump stored NEQs as intervals */\n 1075 | \tuint base = num;\n 1076 | \tfor (uint i = 0; i < neqs; i++)\n 1077 | \t{\n 1078 | \t  val = buf[base + i][0];\n 1079 | \n 1080 | \t  if ((val < lo) || (val > hi))\n 1081 | \t    continue;\n 1082 | \n 1083 | \t  if (val == lo)\n 1084 | \t  { lo++; continue; }\n 1085 | \n 1086 | \t  if (val == hi)\n 1087 | \t  { hi--; continue; }\n 1088 | \n 1089 | \t  buf[num][0] = lo;\n 1090 | \t  buf[num][1] = val - 1;\n 1091 | \t  num++;\n 1092 | \n 1093 | \t  lo = val + 1;\n 1094 | \t}\n 1095 | \n 1096 | \tneqs = 0;\n 1097 |       }\n 1098 | \n 1099 |       /* Save final interval */\n 1100 |       if (lo <= hi)\n 1101 |       {\n 1102 | \tbuf[num][0] = lo;\n 1103 | \tbuf[num][1] = hi;\n 1104 | \tnum++;\n 1105 |       }\n 1106 | \n 1107 |       lo = 0;\n 1108 |       hi = max;\n 1109 |     }\n 1110 | \n 1111 |     if (last)\n 1112 |       break;\n 1113 | \n 1114 |     pos = next_pos;\n 1115 |   }\n 1116 | \n 1117 |   if (num < 2)\n 1118 |     return num;\n 1119 | \n 1120 |   /* Step 2 - Sort and merge list of intervals */\n 1121 |   qsort(buf, num, 2 * sizeof(uint), flow_uint_cmp);\n 1122 | \n 1123 |   uint i = 0, j = 0;\n 1124 |   while (i < num)\n 1125 |   {\n 1126 |     lo = buf[i][0];\n 1127 |     hi = buf[i][1];\n 1128 |     i++;\n 1129 | \n 1130 |     /* If intervals are intersecting or just touching, merge them */\n 1131 |     while ((i < num) && ((s64) buf[i][0] <= (hi + 1)))\n 1132 |     {\n 1133 |       hi = MAX(hi, (s64) buf[i][1]);\n 1134 |       i++;\n 1135 |     }\n 1136 | \n 1137 |     buf[j][0] = lo;\n 1138 |     buf[j][1] = hi;\n 1139 |     j++;\n 1140 |   }\n 1141 | \n 1142 |   return j;\n 1143 | }\n 1144 | \n 1145 | \n 1146 | /*\n 1147 |  * \tNet Formatting\n 1148 |  */\n 1149 | \n 1150 | /* Flowspec operators for [op, value]+ pairs */\n 1151 | \n 1152 | static const char *\n 1153 | num_op_str(const byte *op)\n 1154 | {\n 1155 |   switch (*op & 0x07)\n 1156 |   {\n 1157 |   case FLOW_OP_TRUE:\treturn \"true\";\n 1158 |   case FLOW_OP_EQ:\treturn \"=\";\n 1159 |   case FLOW_OP_GT:\treturn \">\";\n 1160 |   case FLOW_OP_GEQ:\treturn \">=\";\n 1161 |   case FLOW_OP_LT:\treturn \"<\";\n 1162 |   case FLOW_OP_LEQ:\treturn \"<=\";\n 1163 |   case FLOW_OP_NEQ:\treturn \"!=\";\n 1164 |   case FLOW_OP_FALSE:\treturn \"false\";\n 1165 |   }\n 1166 | \n 1167 |   return NULL;\n 1168 | }\n 1169 | \n 1170 | static const char *\n 1171 | fragment_val_str(u8 val)\n 1172 | {\n 1173 |   switch (val)\n 1174 |   {\n 1175 |   case 1: return \"dont_fragment\";\n 1176 |   case 2: return \"is_fragment\";\n 1177 |   case 4: return \"first_fragment\";\n 1178 |   case 8: return \"last_fragment\";\n 1179 |   }\n 1180 |   return \"???\";\n 1181 | }\n 1182 | \n 1183 | static void\n 1184 | net_format_flow_ip(buffer *b, const byte *part, int ipv6)\n 1185 | {\n 1186 |   uint pxlen = part[1];\n 1187 |   if (ipv6)\n 1188 |   {\n 1189 |     uint pxoffset = part[2];\n 1190 |     if (pxoffset)\n 1191 |       buffer_print(b, \"%I6/%u 1197 |     buffer_print(b, \"%I4/%u; \", flow_read_ip4_part(part), pxlen);\n 1198 |   }\n 1199 | }\n 1200 | \n 1201 | static void\n 1202 | net_format_flow_num(buffer *b, const byte *part)\n 1203 | {\n 1204 |   const byte *last_op = NULL;\n 1205 |   const byte *op = part+1;\n 1206 |   uint val;\n 1207 |   uint len;\n 1208 |   uint first = 1;\n 1209 | \n 1210 |   while (1)\n 1211 |   {\n 1212 |     if (!first)\n 1213 |     {\n 1214 |       /* XXX: I don't like this so complicated if-tree */\n 1215 |       if (!isset_and(op) &&\n 1216 | \t  ((num_op(     op) == FLOW_OP_EQ) || (num_op(     op) == FLOW_OP_GEQ)) &&\n 1217 | \t  ((num_op(last_op) == FLOW_OP_EQ) || (num_op(last_op) == FLOW_OP_LEQ)))\n 1218 |       {\n 1219 | \tb->pos--; /* Remove last char (it is a space) */\n 1220 | \tbuffer_puts(b, \",\");\n 1221 |       }\n 1222 |       else\n 1223 |       {\n 1224 | \tbuffer_puts(b, isset_and(op) ? \"&& \" : \"|| \");\n 1225 |       }\n 1226 |     }\n 1227 |     first = 0;\n 1228 | \n 1229 |     len = get_value_length(op);\n 1230 |     val = get_value(op+1, len);\n 1231 | \n 1232 |     if (!isset_end(op) && !isset_and(op) && isset_and(op+1+len) &&\n 1233 | \t(num_op(op) == FLOW_OP_GEQ) && (num_op(op+1+len) == FLOW_OP_LEQ))\n 1234 |     {\n 1235 |       /* Display interval */\n 1236 |       buffer_print(b, \"%u..\", val);\n 1237 |       op += 1 + len;\n 1238 |       len = get_value_length(op);\n 1239 |       val = get_value(op+1, len);\n 1240 |       buffer_print(b, \"%u\", val);\n 1241 |     }\n 1242 |     else if (num_op(op) == FLOW_OP_EQ)\n 1243 |     {\n 1244 |       buffer_print(b, \"%u\", val);\n 1245 |     }\n 1246 |     else\n 1247 |     {\n 1248 |       buffer_print(b, \"%s %u\", num_op_str(op), val);\n 1249 |     }\n 1250 | \n 1251 |     if | \n 1261 |     last_op = op;\n 1262 |     op += 1 + len;\n 1263 |   }\n 1264 | }\n 1265 | \n 1266 | static void\n 1267 | net_format_flow_bitmask(buffer *b, const byte *part)\n 1268 | {\n 1269 |   const byte *op = part+1;\n 1270 |   uint val;\n 1271 |   uint len;\n 1272 |   uint first = 1;\n 1273 | \n 1274 |   while (1)\n 1275 |   {\n 1276 |     if (!first)\n 1277 |       buffer_puts(b,  *   Not Match  Show\n 1286 |      *  ------------------\n 1287 |      *    0    0    !0/B\n 1288 |      *    0    1     B/B\n 1289 |      *    1    0     0/B\n 1290 |      *    1    1    !B/B\n 1291 |      */\n 1292 | \n 1293 |     if ((*op & 0x3) == 0x3 || (*op & 0x3) == 0)\n 1294 |       buffer_puts(b, \"!\");\n 1295 | \n 1296 |     if (*part == FLOW_TYPE_FRAGMENT && (val == 1 || val == 2 ||
